programme.title: Meet the Penguins

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: A dead horse is found at a farm in northern Pennsylvania, where 13 other horses are living in dreadful conditions. Plus, an emaciated dog and a cat with a large tumour are treated. Documentary following the work of the SPCA's Humane Law Enforcement team, which investigates cases of animal cruelty

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Twelve investigators have police back-up on a dangerous assignment to arrest a dog breeder selling sick puppies

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees

programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps Orange County housewife Peggy, whose cat's behaviour is getting out of hand

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Wardens Mitchell and Lindsey intervene when an overloaded boat and unruly jet skiers put lives at risk. Plus, wardens round up a wily alligator

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Rugged Justice

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: The team pulls out all the stops to save a newborn foal when it is found with its abandoned mother

programme.desc: A hyperactive Persian cat suddenly collapses while playing, and Jackson sees if a pet that has been exiled from the family home is ready to come back indoors

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas. She also reveals how heat is the hidden energy contained within matter, with the power to transform it from one state to another

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the second semi-final, in which the Bankers face the Lapsed Psychologists for a place in the grand finale

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Part two of two. Wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, Slumdog Millionaire actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta explore the natural world in India, including a village of birds, masks that come alive, an impressive mountain range and baby turtles erupting out of the sand

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the first of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two teams of students get a second opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the final of the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. The Crossworders battle the Lapsed Pyschologists to be crowned series victors

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas. She also reveals how heat is the hidden energy contained within matter, with the power to transform it from one state to another

programme.title: Murder on the Victorian Railway
programme.desc: Drama-documentary examining the death of banker Thomas Briggs in July 1864, which was the first murder to be committed on a British train. The film uses testimonies from court transcripts, memoirs, letters and newspaper reports to tell the story of the crime through those connected to it, including engine driver Alfred Ekin - who found the body - and Richard Tanner, the detective in charge of the investigation. It also explores the huge public outcry and debate it provoked. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Stephen wins the confidence of the notorious Professor John Furie - until a combination of drink and drugs drives his patient into murderous paranoia. Peter Davison and Timothy West star

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Bob Buzzard tests a pharmaceutical company's new wonder drug on all his patients, causing a rash of bizarre side-effects around the campus. David Troughton and Peter Davison star in the surreal university drama

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: How to Write a Mills & Boon
programme.desc: Author Stella Duffy sets out to produce her own novel for Mills & Boon, the company that has become one of the biggest names in romantic fiction. She enlists the help of some avid fans in a challenging mission that takes her from London to Italy and offers an insight into the joy and frustration of writing

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the final of the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. The Crossworders battle the Lapsed Pyschologists to be crowned series victors

programme.title: Murder on the Victorian Railway
programme.desc: Drama-documentary examining the death of banker Thomas Briggs in July 1864, which was the first murder to be committed on a British train. The film uses testimonies from court transcripts, memoirs, letters and newspaper reports to tell the story of the crime through those connected to it, including engine driver Alfred Ekin - who found the body - and Richard Tanner, the detective in charge of the investigation. It also explores the huge public outcry and debate it provoked. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the second of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two teams of students get a second opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Oxford Librarians
programme.desc: Three men from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford librarians to try and make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: The story of the Hollywood female film icon, told through insights and anecdotes of friends, directors and fellow actors, who spoke about her over the years on a selection of BBC programmes. With archive interviews featuring stars such as Jane Russell, Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, as well as directors like Billy Wilder and John Huston, and Marilyn's former husband Arthur Miller

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Nobody's Perfect: The Making of Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: Stars Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, together with other members of the cast and crew, recall the making of Some Like It Hot. Featuring rare colour footage from behind the scenes, providing an intimate portrait of Billy Wilder's classic Hollywood comedy

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: Jazz 625
programme.desc: Archive material from the show originally broadcast in the 1960s, with performances by Duke Ellington, Dave Brubeck, Dizzy Gillespie and the Julian 'Cannonball' Adderley Sextet. Plus, rarely seen sessions featuring the likes of Wes Montgomery, Marian McPartland, Chris Barber's Jazz Band and Willie 'The Lion' Smith

programme.title: The Prince, the Showgirl and Me
programme.desc: Colin Clark uses his diaries written during the making of 1956 romantic comedy The Prince and the Showgirl to chronicle the gradual breakdown of relations between the film's stars Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Oxford Librarians
programme.desc: Three men from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford librarians to try and make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his exploration of the artistic history of the northern European region in Sweden - home of IKEA - exploring the country's tradition of furniture design

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Highlights from series 19 of the comedy panel show hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. With appearances by Lucy Beaumont, Bob Mortimer, Jamelia, John Kearns, Harriet Kemsley, Yinka Bokinni, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Mark Chapman, Montell Douglas, Tasha Ghouri, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Will Kirk, David Morrissey, Josh Pugh and Holly Willoughby

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Help! Is My Online Job a Scam?
programme.desc: The team help a viewer concerned that the online task work he has been completing might not be genuine. They also have an update on an undercover report into a dodgy spray-foam insulation firm, and how their footage helped put the man responsible behind bars

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is overcome by the amount of beige on show in a Telford flat. Dion Dublin heads downwards to reveal an unexpected bonus in Leeds. In Suffolk, there are character features for Jacqui Joseph to uncover

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Fergus and Caroline Hawley guide the teams at an antiques centre in King's Lynn, Norfolk. Charlie Ross pays a visit to a barbershop museum

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: As Natalie juggles college and family life, she learns about teenage mental health, online pressures and autism - and how care reaches far beyond the classroom. She also realises she has been putting off her own coursework, and as she finally sits down to start it, she gains a better understanding of the pressures facing her teenage daughter Eliza

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles joins Robson to explore the Northumberland coastline, with the pair taking a Canadian canoe trip along the Coquet river and visiting the hidden gem of Warkworth's secret hermitage. They also make the crossing to Lindisfarne for a photography class and have a go at rope work

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps Micky, who wants to relocate from the busy seaside town of Hove in East Sussex to the quiet of the Somerset countryside. She wants a property with lots of land for her big, beloved rescue dog, so Jules lines up a selection of standout houses that might just fit the bill. The presenter also tries his hand at traditional screen printing when he visits a small studio specialising in designs inspired by nature

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Florence Headlam head to Shrewsbury to help James regenerate his plain garden into a Doctor Who-inspired space, complete with Tardis shed, futuristic planting, a circular pathway and a tranquil water feature. With the support of James's sister Maria, and Charlie and Florence's creativity, it isn't long before this ordinary garden is turned into somewhere extraordinary where sci-fi fan James can feel truly at home

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Anna, Gemma, Zoe and Andreas competes for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Ria Lina

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and JB Gill present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It is the day of Vicki and Ross's wedding, but it remains to be seen whether it goes ahead, and Priya gets a shock

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals kick off at the National Railway Museum in York, where the contestants cater for a banquet to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the birth of the modern railway. But fine-dining on this scale, for 100 guests, is beyond anything the amateurs have ever faced before - and to add to the pressure, the menu has been designed by judge Anna Haugh, who is running the service

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds out a big house on her street is owned by an elderly lady who is planning to move out, so she inserts herself into her life, hoping to get first refusal on the sale. Lucy Punch stars, with a guest appearance by Pam Ferris

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken forces Richard to accompany him to Aviemore so that he can help him navigate the QR code scanner at the train station ticket barrier. However, a mix-up on the platform turns what should be a simple day trip into an almighty fiasco

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl visits an amateur MMA fighter on probation whose sporting dreams have been put on ice after a historic conviction for assault against a former partner has resurfaced. Carl also meets a man who is getting his forklift licence - before he can even drive a car - and finds out how support from probation, and his peers, is helping him build a new life

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Rugby player Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johannson must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Was Part of the World's Largest Scam
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Louise Minchin and Julia Somerville unite the victim of an international investment scam network with one of the investigative journalists who uncovered it. At least 32,000 people lost more than £200 million in the crime. Plus, how purchasing gift cards on someone else's behalf can leave someone vulnerable to being scammed, and the Liverpool fan who was left empty-handed when he tried to buy tickets from an unofficial seller

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui Joseph offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine presents from Naseby Antiques Fair, Northamptonshire, where experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks offer up their expertise to assist two teams as they search for profitable items to take to auction. Roo also brings along a mysterious antique to quiz the locals on what they think it is

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie is halfway through her college course and growing in confidence. She meets up with her EastEnders colleague Kitty Castledine, who talks about life using a wheelchair since childhood, giving Natalie a clearer sense of the realities and barriers that come with accessibility. On placement, she enters the home of Lisa, who has multiple sclerosis, and her partner Gary, who cares for her

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by Invictus Games gold medal winner JJ Chalmers. The pair head to Killicrankie, where they zip wire across the River Garry, before heading to their digs for the night. They also take a sledge ride through countryside and then head to the Blackruthven Estate, where they are given a drystone-walling lesson

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps members of a young family who have outgrown their London flat and now need a new home in rural Suffolk with enough room for two growing children. The exciting property line-up includes a mystery house that has six centuries of stories to tell - some written on its walls. Briony is also excited to meet a fellow baking enthusiast who, after making his own escape to the county a year ago, now sells cakes and runs a supper club from his home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Diarmuid Gavin are in Croydon to help sisters Lynette and Natalie rescue their vast, sun-baked plot where weeds have taken over, vegetable beds are impossible to reach and a polytunnel and pallets leave little room for peace. The sisters dream of a peaceful retreat where they can reconnect with nature. Can the team transform this cluttered space for £5,500?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Louise, Emily, Laura and Beth compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones - making sure they avoid the lies. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Grace Dent present the second of the semi-final rounds, challenging the amateurs to serve up one dish inspired by someone special. The most successful cooks then move on to the second challenge - a feast to mark the 120th anniversary of the landmark building Whitehall, the former war office for military and secret service operations - for seven special guests who all hail from the world of espionage

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Documentary following attempts to find missing people, beginning with police racing to find two teenagers struggling with their mental health. Sixteen-year-old Larissa disappeared after an argument with her mum, while 18-year-old Bobby may be at risk of suicide

programme.desc: Nida offers to get Mobeen a fake passport if he can steal something from the Turks. Will help from Uncle Shady and his big drill be enough to pull it off? Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Tensions run high as the gang debates what to do with the stolen money. Jerry reveals his true colours, Emre investigates the theft, and Uncle Shady offers Mobeen a lifeline. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: On a plane alone, Mobeen recounts how it all started, with his teenage memories of the first job he did for Khan and the beginning of his brotherhood with Nate and Eight. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Police connect the suicide of a 12-year-old girl in West Virginia to Alexander McCartney, opening a manslaughter investigation but in order to charge him they have to prove he foresaw the risk to her life. The case sets a new legal precedent in the UK and more than five years after the investigation began, McCartney is finally convicted and sentenced

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A man afflicted with chronic bad luck tries to reconnect with his dad after setting fire to the family home. Comedy short, starring Dylan Blore and Mark Bonnar

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two emotionally unassembled young men start a play group, Funboys Fun Club, for childish young men to engage in some wholesome mucking about. When they come across severely depressed 60-year-old Marty, they try their best to bring some joy back to his life. Pilot for the sitcom, with Rian Lennon and Ryan Early

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker revisits documentaries exploring the commodification of forbidden desires, taking in subjects ranging from the porn industry to the US opioid crisis. He is reunited with porn star JJ, who has turned his back on his old career to begin a new life in eastern Europe, and drug addict Katilia, who is still struggling to turn her life around

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Zambia to witness the world's largest aerial migration. However, these 10 million fruit bats are nothing but food for the eagles of Africa

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Sydney, Australia, searching for the world's most venomous spider, the fastest striking snake, and an ocean dragon that's a camouflaged assassin

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the teams heading to the literary town of Hay-on-Wye in Wales, where they work in pairs to transform two high street shops. One sells guitars, the other arts and crafts, and the aim of the designers is to improve layout and visual merchandising to entice more customers. Alan follows their progress, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by retail expert Ross Bailey to assess the final results

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend
programme.desc: The best moments from the music event at Sunderland's Herrington Country Park, featuring headliners Fatboy Slim, Zara Larsson and Olivia Dean

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Sitcom about a reformed drug dealer trying to look after his younger sister, despite his murky past constantly catching up with him. Mobeen's plan to get to Aqsa goes wrong, but before he can accept Uncle Shady's drastic solution, a familiar face shows up with an offer of their own. Guz Khan and Mark Silcox star

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Two burglars break into a house, where they come across a man already tied up. Comedy short, written by and starring Stephen Buchanan and Martin Quinn

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend
programme.desc: The best moments from the music event at Sunderland's Herrington Country Park, featuring headliners Fatboy Slim, Zara Larsson and Olivia Dean

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: In Turkey, Istanbul offers many distractions, and Mobeen struggles to keep the gang focused on their mission to rescue Aqsa and kill Khan. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen and the gang face Khan in a final showdown. Can Mobeen save Aqsa and his friends to get back home to the ends? Final episode of the comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: On a Bradford estate, Lenny and Mullet find an abandoned car and turn it into a DIY spaceship. Elif Riley, Logan Ludbrook, Lucy Francis and Hayley Brown star in this kitchen-sink sci-fi comedy about big dreams, everyday lives and escaping without going anywhere

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: David Attenborough introduces footage of animals in Asia that live at extremely close quarters with people, from tigers making a home in town to elephants stopping traffic for food. A small park in central Bangkok supports 300 giant lizards, whilst a flying squirrel in Taipei has made a cosy home in a school. Proboscis monkeys are forced to venture uncomfortably closer to humans, and swifts in Jerusalem face tough competition for nest sites

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. Arriving in Vietnam, they are challenged to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days. First they must buy suitable transport for the journey, and despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves more difficult than they expected, especially in one of the most expensive car markets in the world

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special edition of the motoring show, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Africa on a mission to find the definitive source of the Nile, the world's longest river, which spans more than 4,000 miles. The trio believe they can do a better job than explorers who claim to have discovered the waterway's starting point by travelling further and faster than previous efforts - but the only tools at their disposal are determination, ingenuity and three ageing estate cars

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part Two
programme.desc: After conquering traffic, dangerous mud slides and dubious map reading, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May conclude their quest to find the definitive source of the River Nile in Africa. The trio use a small raft to cross crocodile-infested waters and progress to Lake Victoria, where they hope to find a boat that will take them to the vast plains of the Serengeti. Then, after hundreds of punishing miles in their tired estate cars, the petrolheads make one final push to home in on their target

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Radio presenter Sian Eleri investigates the paranormal activity at Penyffordd Farm in north Wales, the house being widely considered the most haunted house in Britain in the late 1990s. She begins by meeting with specialist psychologist Dr Daniels who was called in by the family living on the farm at the time when they became desperate for answers for the strange activity in their home. The doctor explains that of the many apparently paranormal cases of his career, this was the one that stayed with him and still perplexes him to this day

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the world's biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs' inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott visits Moscow, Idaho to uncover the story of the social media frenzy that followed the murders of four Idaho University students. She meets with online true crime sleuths building their own theories about the murders and those who were personally affected by the social media storm as she reflects on her own role in the tragedy and learns more about obsessive online consumption of true crime

programme.title: The Fast and the Farmer-ish
programme.desc: The teams compete in the semi-final, where they must escape from a vault, display an artistic side and succeed in a super-sized tractor driving challenge. One team thinks it's all over before they even start, but there are twists ahead, with places in the final at stake

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient's tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two dog-loving developers tackle epic projects in Wales and Norfolk. A seasoned developer is looking to move quickly and economically in Nottingham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Jacqui Joseph

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian rail journey continues in the Dutch speaking region of Flanders. In Antwerp, he discovers the city's historic multi-billion-pound diamond industry and drops in at the workshop of an expert. Heading to Belgium's third city Ghent, he visits the cathedral and marvels at a 15th-century Van Eyck masterpiece, the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo takes a luxury, panoramic Shimikaze train to the Shima peninsula. It transports visitors to Ise Jingu, Japan's most sacred Shinto shrine. The next stop is Nara, the ancient capital of Japan, a tranquil city where some 1,400 sika deer roam the green open space. He also visits the Buddhist Todaiji Temple, where he sees one of the largest bronze Buddha statues in the world

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Leaving Seoul aboard South Korea's high-speed KTX train, Michael Portillo travels south through the heart of the country to explore how rail has shaped both its economy and identity. His first stop is Daejeon, a centre of science and education, where a local bakery founded by a North Korean refugee offers a small but telling story of post-war resilience

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer question on the specialist subjects of the life of the films of jazz pianist Bill Evans, Georges Remi and Tintin, William Morris, and Torchwood, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Daisy struggles to get everyone back on track to complete the showcase preparations while still hurting over her argument with Griffin. Louis receives a distressing phone call and finds himself roped in to taking part in a rabbit hunt. Marissa ups her competitive game

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: Documentary following the emotional and dramatic lives of elephants in Kenya's Samburu reserve. There is excitement in one family when a calf is born, but the infant's first few weeks look set to be the most dangerous of its life. Father-and-daughter team Iain and Saba Douglas-Hamilton also have a big challenge - fitting a radio collar to a three-ton female. Narrated by Sarah Parish

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the experts continue their visit to Warwick Castle, and cast their eyes over more treasures, including the strangest cigar lighter ever seen on the programme, a model guillotine made by French PoWs and a life drawing of remarkable quality

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At St Albans Cathedral and Abbey, Hertfordshire, Claire Rawle discovers an arts and crafts bowl that may have belonged to George Bernard Shaw's wife, and Jonathan Pratt finds a drawing by the artist Laura Knight. Presenter Paul Martin heads underground as he explores Royston Cave, which boasts medieval carvings

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his Belgian tour, Michael Portillo heads to the beach resort of Knokke-Heist and the world's longest coastal tram, which enjoys views across the North Sea. In the medieval city of Bruges, he visits a saxophone maker and discovers how the instrument, so crucial to the development of jazz, was invented in Belgium

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the wildlife of the Dee valley, from wintering birds along the estuary, to displaying black grouse on moorland, rare freshwater-pearl mussels, and spawning sea trout

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah explores whether the death of privacy puts society at greater risk or could make people safer

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry delves into the mysteries of modern technology to uncover the fascinating stories behind the everyday objects that people take for granted

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and the team continue to broadcast live from the National Trust's Crom Estate as nature writes its own stories. The live remote cameras are capturing fledging, predation and drama around the clock, from otters on the lough shore to red squirrels in ancient woodlands. Iolo Williams takes a look at the lives of some of the country's rarest species at the Knepp rewilding estate in Sussex

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons drain all of their patient's blood from his body to replace damaged arteries. A patient's fertility is at risk without a complex operation to remove diseased bowel

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: On patrol in Belfast city centre Adam pursues a fleeing suspect on foot. A few minutes later, he must do it again, as the man bursts out of the police car, trying to escape. Back at the station, Anna and Perri can't resist the temptation to hide the boss's car, temporarily. Meanwhile, the whole team leap into action when a stolen car fails to stop

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBC's world affairs editor reflects on the week's major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: Dark Waters (2019)
programme.desc: When a defence attorney takes on a lawsuit case against a chemical company, he finds a deep and rich trial in their history that unravels a history of pollution. Drama, starring Mark Ruffalo, Anne Hathaway, Tim Robbins, Bill Pullman and Bill Camp

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week continues, and the second set of seven amateur cooks must prove themselves enough to make it into their first professional kitchen. First they must prove to judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent they have what it takes by serving up a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook, with only the first best getting the chance to deliver a lunchtime service at the Straits Kitchen restaurant at London's Pan Pacific Hotel

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week begins with the first seven of the 14 remaining cooks given the chance to work in their first professional kitchen. But first, Anna Haugh and Grace Dent ask them to deliver a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook. For two, this will be the end of the road, while the others spend the day at the renowned Roe restaurant in Canary Wharf

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: OK Computer, How Do I Feel?
programme.desc: Hannah Fry explores whether artificial intelligence read emotions better than a human, as well as the new technology creeping into people's lives

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Derry/Londonderry

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is overcome by the amount of beige on show in a Telford flat. Dion Dublin heads downwards to reveal an unexpected bonus in Leeds. In Suffolk, there are character features for Jacqui Joseph to uncover

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his Belgian tour, Michael Portillo heads to the beach resort of Knokke-Heist and the world's longest coastal tram, which enjoys views across the North Sea. In the medieval city of Bruges, he visits a saxophone maker and discovers how the instrument, so crucial to the development of jazz, was invented in Belgium

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part one of two. The historian examines the turning points that led to America's declaration of independence from Britain in 1776 and asks whether this explosive split could have been avoided. The eight-year war that followed claimed thousands of lives and ended in a humiliating defeat for George III, and Lucy sets out to uncover why America chose this path and how Britain's missteps fractured an empire and shook the monarchy

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair. They answer question on the specialist subjects of Tudor rebellions, Christina Aguilera, the US Space Shuttle programme, and James Parkinson, before being tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Oakley Wines enjoys new-found fame and welcomes a surprise visitor, while gala preparations take an explosive turn, putting the entire showcase at risk. Drama, starring Rebecca Gibney and Charles Edwards

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: Calf Breeze faces her biggest test yet as the dry season approaches, while teenage brother Buster seems intent on getting both of them into trouble. Iain Douglas-Hamilton sends daughter Saba on a mission to help one of the reserve's best-known elephants, Mountain Bull, who has been on a collision course with farmers and local villagers. Sarah Parish narrates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel introduces more treasures from a visit to Harrogate, North Yorkshire. Featuring a table made from an ancient bedpost, a manuscript diary written by a woman who toured Great Britain in 1815, and an interesting example of scrimshaw

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Grimsby Minster in Lincolnshire, where Anita Manning and Michael Baggott assess maritime memorabilia including a ship's bell and a diver's knife. Paul Martin meets a Grimsby trawlerman on the very vessel he took to sea

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges ahead of Friday's final

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian journey takes him back to Brussels to examine a dark chapter in the country's past. At the Museum of Africa, on the edge of the city, he learns about Belgium's brutal seizure of the Congo under King Leopold II and a grand exhibition to celebrate his colonial success. Travelling south into French-speaking Wallonia, Michael visits the site of the Battle of Waterloo, one of Britain's most famous military victories

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Richard Osman and Ingrid Oliver search for profitable antiques in Norfolk, joined by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram. Raj and Ingrid have high hopes for some expensive West German pottery, but Richard and Natasha are discombobulated when their budget buy of a Scandinavian glass ashtray turns out to be a case of mistaken identity

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The pressure builds as chicks prepare to fledge and predators close in. The team hope to capture even more intimate moments from the lives of the wildlife calling National Trust Crom home through their 24-hour remote camera surveillance. Away from Crom, Iolo Williams spends his final night live at Knepp, where rewilding is transforming the landscape

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Return of the programme that explores the lives of notable people, beginning with a look at Russian president Vladimir Putin's rise to power through 10 defining photos, examining how he has used imagery, TV and the media to control the narrative and consolidate his power. The film charts Putin's progress from an unremarkable childhood in Leningrad (now St Petersburg) to KGB agent in Dresden in East Germany, where the fall of the Berlin Wall and the collapse of the Soviet Union two years later changed everything

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl visits an amateur MMA fighter on probation whose sporting dreams have been put on ice after a historic conviction for assault against a former partner has resurfaced. Carl also meets a man who is getting his forklift licence - before he can even drive a car - and finds out how support from probation, and his peers, is helping him build a new life

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Katie Razzall

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Acting on a tip-off, the Peelers storm a city centre flat on a drugs bust. Inside, a suspect is startled, cuffed and arrested. Outside, the PSNI team search the nearby streets for the suspected 'big-time' dealer they think may have fled the scene with more drugs. In Belfast city centre, police attempt to arrest a man who refuses to comply with a search and the situation quickly escalates

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.sub-title: Blakeney Point - Bass and Sea Trout
programme.desc: Bob, Paul and Ted travel to Blakeney Point, a nature reserve and important nesting ground for terns and gulls, for a sea-fishing adventure off the north Norfolk coast. On the first day, the rain is lashing down, but they bravely meet the elements head on and fish in some of the most challenging conditions they have ever faced. After a day of mixed fortunes, they take a boat back to the car and head to their accommodation for the evening. Bear's Cottage was built in 1856 and is surrounded by 200 acres of trees, including giant redwoods and noble firs

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: As knockout week comes to a close, the remaining 10 chefs are tasked with making the perfect pizza, but with only four ovens between them they have some negotiating to do. Guest judge James Elliot, co-founder of Pizza Pilgrims, must choose the best four to send them straight to the semi-final. The last six chefs are challenged to make a dish inspired by their favourite takeaway

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers travel to Leeds, where they must transform studio apartments, creating cleverly divided open-plan spaces to entice potential renters in Leeds. Alan follows their progress throughout, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by former winner Lynsey Ford to critique the final results, before another hopeful is asked to pack up their design kit

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Response officers Anna and Perri race to the aid of a man who has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has a left a pedestrian badly injured

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Drama, starring Jessica Raine. Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But her husband Dan's dark secret threatens to ruin everything, while a hedonistic first night party sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui Joseph offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's Belgian journey takes him back to Brussels to examine a dark chapter in the country's past. At the Museum of Africa, on the edge of the city, he learns about Belgium's brutal seizure of the Congo under King Leopold II and a grand exhibition to celebrate his colonial success. Travelling south into French-speaking Wallonia, Michael visits the site of the Battle of Waterloo, one of Britain's most famous military victories

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.sub-title: The Duke, His Wife and Their Lover
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain's great houses - and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: A look at colossal wonders scattered across the Earth's surface, including an ancient reptile skeleton, a massive head carving in the Alberta badlands, a giant South African footprint, and a pentagram etched into a remote peninsula in Kazakhstan

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Experts examine new evidence which suggests contact with interstellar life forms in the distant past may have influenced many sacred sites across the globe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Witch's Broomstick, UFOs vs Volcanoes and Oakville Blobs
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film', including a witch's broomstick 'floating' in mid-air and fossilised footprints found in Texas. Plus, a look at what might have caused gelatinous blobs of rain to fall from the sky and UFOs purportedly using volcanoes as hidden bases of operations

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid snap up another electric haul at the Tewkesbury auction before heading to the Tettenhall Transport Museum

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les gets excited over a jar of powdered gold as he believes it is worth a lot of money, and an explosive acid test is poised to reveal all

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A customer calls in their lawyer to settle a row over a fake receipt, but Les has a trick up his sleeve

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley challenges Seth to a unique competition in the shop, and should he lose, the one thing he has been trying to keep a secret will be revealed

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

Meanwhile, antiques expert Tony Geering attempts to buy an Asprey powder compact, and a woman wants a tidy sum for her Moroccan watercolour

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the antiques dealers visit Ilkeston in Derbyshire, where Alison Chapman evaluates a Treasure Island illustration, and former soldier Walter shows his gold sovereigns to Simon Schneider. With Tony Geering and Karen Dalmeny

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The team is offered antique photographs of outlaw Jesse James, and an amplifier for a 1950s organ

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick struggles to maintain his tough negotiating skills when offered a 1974 Volkswagen, and the team examines a Second World War fighter plane

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items bound for a tip in Witley, including a pair of old car seats, a collection of glass laboratory tubes and a box of knitting wool

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid hit the road in search and automotive junk to sell, starting at the HJ Pugh auction

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.sub-title: Welcome to the World of the Sonny Monday
programme.desc: The buyers meet their match in Waxahachie's go-getting locals. Will such stiff competition prevent the team members managing to get their hands on any of the goodies?

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: An auction in Mesquite brings back bad memories for Ricky and Bubba, and Mary makes a strange discovery that leads her to a llama farm

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Satellites orbiting the planet spotlight Earth's vast, unexplored areas of land and sea - revealing baffling new anomalies

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: Queens of Ancient Egypt
