programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition features stories of human compassion and kindness in the most challenging circumstances. Allen, 82, has injured his chest after a dizzy spell caused him to fall down 13 steps. As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Chaos reigns in a hotel as one person is accused of assaulting another, and the police are called in while paramedics try to treat both parties at the same time. A trip over a pet dog leaves a woman with a wound on her arm that won't stop bleeding. Plus, a 76-year-old woman with fluctuating blood pressure confides in the paramedics that she's grieving the loss of her husband

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Camping holidaymakers Sugapuff and Therryi, Nik and Harriet, and Emmy and Simon pitch up at a working farm on the edge of Ilkley Moor, West Yorkshire. Shearing sheep and feeding lambs are among the new experiences for this crowd of campsite critics

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The programme heads to Lowestoft in Suffolk, where Henry Cole picks a valuable Lyons Tea enamel sign while Simon O'Brien converts a station trolley into a garden sofa

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The duo rummage through the hangar of a Vulcan bomber, where Henry finds a cockpit seat to restore, and Simon attempts to turn part of a jet engine into a coffee table

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Pencader, Carmarthenshire, at the School House B&B, owned by eco-friendly married couple Jayne and Ian Hall-Edwards, who hope that their environmental approach will impress their guests. At dinner, Ian reveals that his hosting skills might not be up to scratch and eyebrows are raised at straight-talking colleagues Jo and Neil. At breakfast, assumptions are made of businessman Raj

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Cathedral 73 in Cardiff, managed by Neil Chidzey. On arrival, guests are wowed by the glamorous rooms, but Raj is shocked to find breakfast is not included

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Leafy Suburban B&B in Northwood, Hertfordshire, where the guests spend the afternoon taking a swing at some golfing

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Wellington Inn, Hertfordshire, where Felicity Rainbow takes a spiritual approach, cleansing the aura of the rooms and encouraging guests to feel the vibe

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The School House B&B is first and host Jayne Hall-Edwards takes guests back to basics over her green policy. Next, Neil Chidzey and Jo Goodwin from Cathedral 73 challenge their guests over costings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition heads to Manchester, where battling it out for the £1,000 cash prize are part-time DJ Dom, 35-year old barber Danny, trainee barrister Sasha, nurse Katherine and goth Maria. In the first edition, DJ Dom kicks off the week serving up a menu inspired by the 1990s 'Madchester' music scene

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee barrister Sasha hosts the second night in Manchester, where she hopes to impress her guests with a Chinese-Pakistani fusion menu. Meanwhile, fellow contestant Maria reveals more about her love of gothic culture, and Danny discusses his soon-to-be-written autobiography

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Katherine hosts the third dinner party in Manchester, and she opts for a simple menu of a smoked salmon with horseradish cream starter, and a chorizo and chickpea main course. At the table, direct questioning and some extraordinary revelations cause some of the guests to feel uncomfortable

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barber Danny hosts the fourth night of the competition in Manchester, and decides on an evening of Bulgarian cuisine as a homage to his wife. His party ends with some Bulgarian language lessons that bemuse fellow diners Dom and Maria

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final night in Manchester and glamorous goth Maria is hoping to wow her guests with her twist on Asian cuisine. Some of the guests are feeling a little uneasy that the host will impart her love of vampire culture upon her menu, but the blood-free dishes even manage to impress daddy's girl Sasha. However, an avant-garde transgender dancer creates a stir, and at one point it seems possible that Maria has bitten off more than she can chew

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Oaklea Barns Guest House on Merseyside, where Mandi Magee and her partner Paul Green think their impeccable standards will wow their rivals. Guest Ian loves his room but is perplexed by plastic on the lampshades, while Mark and Natalie are thrown by a loose loo. During an exercise in falconry, Dave and Sanchia cry foul when chirpy Mandi rules the roost. At breakfast, Mandi makes it clear she thinks she can't be beaten, but Dave and Sanchia say they'll rise to the challenge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Guest House Worsthorne in Lancashire, where partners Dave Rogers and Sanchia Crossley aim to win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, the group heads to the Sleeperdorm Backpacker Hotel in Newcastle, where Ian Nicholls hopes his guests will enjoy his cheap and cheerful concept

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B battle continues at the Parsonage Hotel and Spa near York, a 55-room country manor which wows the guests with its size, although Mandi's high cleaning standards are not met

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Oaklea Barns is first, and hosts Mandi and Paul want answers from Mark and Natalie, which ends up fanning flames

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple looking for a home in Warrington, who do not seem to be able to agree on the best approach to house hunting, which has led to six months with no progress. In Liverpool, Phil Spencer comes to the aid of a successful entrepreneur looking for a house in Crosby, but who has very specific ideas

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the last chance for players to secure a place in the grand final. Our last set of music fans are put through their paces to find out who has the best pop knowledge

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at six listed places where saving heritage meant a great deal of hard work from ordinary people campaigning, rebuilding, restoring and refusing to give up. The programme features a pioneering Georgian villa outside Edinburgh, Newport Transporter Bridge, a medieval timber hall, and a church saved from the Reformation

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Xintong is rushed to A&E after being flung from a go-kart at speed. Doctors are concerned that there may be damage to the 28 year old's spine and send her for urgent CT scans. Cameras also follow Gary, 58, who has come to St George's after trapping his finger between two shopping trolleys, causing it to swell enormously

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the specialist team dedicated to caring for people when they suffer the potentially devastating consequences of a stroke, including 77-year-old Alex. Husband Paul sits nervously at her bedside as they wait to see if the drug she has been given will bring back her speech and movement. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at six listed places where saving heritage meant a great deal of hard work from ordinary people campaigning, rebuilding, restoring and refusing to give up. The programme features a pioneering Georgian villa outside Edinburgh, Newport Transporter Bridge, a medieval timber hall, and a church saved from the Reformation

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Xintong is rushed to A&E after being flung from a go-kart at speed. Doctors are concerned that there may be damage to the 28 year old's spine and send her for urgent CT scans. Cameras also follow Gary, 58, who has come to St George's after trapping his finger between two shopping trolleys, causing it to swell enormously

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition features stories of human compassion and kindness in the most challenging circumstances. Allen, 82, has injured his chest after a dizzy spell caused him to fall down 13 steps. As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Sitting in the Yorkshire Dales National Park, the campers find themselves at The Dalesbridge where bad weather and a feathery fiasco cause tempers to fray. It remains to be seen whether the stunning scenery and live music will win over the campers as they reveal this week's perfect pitch

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Whitby and Shipley in North and West Yorkshire respectively. Henry builds a steampunk lamp from a copper electric central heating pipe, and Simon brings a dated Welsh dresser right up to the present

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a military vehicle enthusiast, where Henry mirror polishes a decommissioned missile and Simon repurposes a tank turret

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Ship Inn in Dymchurch, Kent, where James Tindall and Jessica Walton hope to impress their guests and be named best value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Thompson Hall Retreat in Norfolk, boasting stylish shepherd huts, and run by mother and daughter duo Amanda Benton and Becci Howlett

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The White House in Swansea, where guests James and Jessica have an unusual toilet encounter in their room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Jacobs Plough in Bicester, Oxfordshire, run by manager Duncan Froomes and bar supervisor Ryan Hunt who like to mix banter with business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid, while also settling some scores

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The culinary contest arrives in Hull as five more diners hope to claim the grand prize of £1,000. The first to host a party this week is 29-year-old welfare team leader Jennie, who decides to honour her home city's fishing history with an under-the-sea themed dinner. However, her guests prove to be a lively bunch, twerking their way through dinner, and throwing in a few seal impressions along the way

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wrestling promoter and novice cook James hosts the second night in Hull, and he hopes to wow his guests with a Mexican-themed party complete with a wrestling match as the evening's entertainment. However, his costume requirements, and his spicy food, may be too much for some of the attendees

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Wedding stationery entrepreneur Katrena hosts the third dinner party in Hull, and hopes to impress her guests with a cruise-ship-themed night featuring a pink strawberry soup starter, a sparkling lemon iceberg dessert and towel-folding entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mum-of-three Natalie hopes that her choice of good quality produce can help her to impress her guests on the fourth night of the competition in Hull, East Yorkshire. However, the evening does not get off to the best start when community manager George wrongly guesses that she is the grandmother of her two-year-old daughter, and entrepreneur Katrena is insulted by one of the host's questions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party in Hull and the turn of community manager George to entertain the guests. Having spent the week heavily criticising everyone else's cooking, the host faces great scrutiny with his menu of simple yet tasty favourites. It remains to be seen whether his egg drop soup and flambeed crepes Suzettes live up to expectations, before the winner of the cash prize is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former art dealers Robin and Brenda Spence host first, at Causilgey Manor in Truro, Cornwall. Leoni and Tish from The Chocolate Box are stumped by the decor, and Julie and Jason from The Buckle Seaford react unfavourably to the bathroom with a glass roof. The B&Bers bond at an owl sanctuary, but cracks begin to appear at dinner when Martin and Tracey of Elmfield B&B come clean with their thoughts on the wall hangings. In the morning, Robin and Brenda show that breakfast isn't all about fry-ups, but Jason and Julie's loo-with-a-view leaves the hosts with egg on their faces

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Elmfield B&B in Devon, where retired politician Martin Perry and housekeeper Tracey Barrett pride themselves on keeping things traditional

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Chocolate Box in Bournemouth, where the hosts playful approach leaves their guests questioning their business credentials

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Buckle Seaford B&B in East Sussex, where an afternoon of football confirms the women's suspicions about hosts Julie and Jason Martin's competitive streak

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, and Causilgey Manor owners Robin and Brenda want to get to grips with Julie and Jason's gripes over privacy

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help house-hunters find their dream properties in the Peak District, guiding them through the complex world of viewings and agents. Kirstie is with single mother Anne, who sold her home in Sheffield to move to the countryside with her teenage son. However, a short-term stay with her parents has turned into eight months, so Kirstie has her work cut out to find Anne a suitable property fast. Phil is with Andrea and James, who are still searching for their ideal place after two years

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Britain's toughest music quiz concludes, as Ken Bruce puts the finalists to the ultimate pop trivia test in the grand final. Who'll reign supreme and be crowned PopMaster TV Champion?

programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates an aerial guide to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway, from the coast at Whitby, across the Yorkshire Moors, to the market town of Pickering. Seen from above, the rail route reveals how George Stephenson's steam revolution changed people, land, industry and trade for ever

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Greg, 47, is airlifted to St George's with critical injuries to his arm and leg after being knocked off his motorbike on the way into work. As his wife Lucie rushes to his bedside, she's shocked by what she finds. Cameras also follow, Cara, 11, who has fallen from the top of a human pyramid doing gymnastics and has a broken arm that doctors must try to realign

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A motorcyclist collides with a deer. Luckily for the patient, an orthopaedic surgeon stops to help. A woman driving to work is forced to pull over and call 999 after experiencing heart palpitations. A young man is constantly being sick and needs to go to hospital. A bike ride ends with a bruised bottom the likes of which the paramedics have never seen before, and after seven weeks with an infection an asthma patient is still struggling to breathe

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Husband Paul sits nervously at her bedside as they wait to see if the drug she has been given will bring back her speech and movement. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Three groups of campers explore The Highlands of Scotland. Their first stop is Aberfeldy Caravan Park on the river Tay, where a ceilidh and some whitewater rafting are on the cards

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin looks at six listed places where saving heritage meant a great deal of hard work from ordinary people campaigning, rebuilding, restoring and refusing to give up. The programme features a pioneering Georgian villa outside Edinburgh, Newport Transporter Bridge, a medieval timber hall, and a church saved from the Reformation

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Forty-year-old Tania is rushed to St George's with life-threatening injuries, having been knocked over while crossing a road, while semi-professional footballer Lilli arrives in A&E after being elbowed in the face during a match. Seventy-seven-year-old Edwin is rushed to resus bleeding from a tumour on his neck, and as the medical team examines the wound, his partner Jeanette explains how their romance blossomed in later life. When a maxillofacial specialist arrives to plan the next stage of Edwin's treatment, it forces his family to confront the very painful truth

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Chaos reigns in a hotel as one person is accused of assaulting another, and the police are called in while paramedics try to treat both parties at the same time. A trip over a pet dog leaves a woman with a wound on her arm that won't stop bleeding. Plus, a 76-year-old woman with fluctuating blood pressure confides in the paramedics that she's grieving the loss of her husband

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Camping holidaymakers Sugapuff and Therryi, Nik and Harriet, and Emmy and Simon pitch up at a working farm on the edge of Ilkley Moor, West Yorkshire. Shearing sheep and feeding lambs are among the new experiences for this crowd of campsite critics

programme.desc: The programme heads to Lowestoft in Suffolk, where Henry Cole picks a valuable Lyons Tea enamel sign while Simon O'Brien converts a station trolley into a garden sofa

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the specialist team dedicated to caring for people when they suffer the potentially devastating consequences of a stroke, including 77-year-old Alex. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation: Special Report
programme.desc: Relying on instinct rather than the rulebook, three flights end in tragedy when crews improvise on their final approach

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: Draining Lake Michigan uncovers Chicago's secret history. Sonar technology exposes a catastrophe that killed 300 people, solves a tragic Christmas mystery, uncovers a game-changing naval training programme from World War II, and reveals an engineering marvel that stops the fastest-growing city in the world from choking to death

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists investigate Egypt's legendary pharaoh, Alexander the Great. The teams explore catacombs and temples, searching for his legacy

programme.title: World War II: Secrets from Above
programme.desc: It's 1944, and the Allies have launched a daring attack on the Nazi-occupied Netherlands in a bid to bring the Second World War to an end

programme.title: Plane Crash Recreated
programme.desc: The story of Air France Flight 447, a scheduled passenger flight from Rio de Janeiro to Paris which dramatically vanished over the Atlantic Ocean in June 2009

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Investigators examine the case of a plane that crashed and burst into flames just moments after take-off, but find that neither the weather nor the runway caused the disaster

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures
programme.desc: How German forces were led into a trap on Sicily by false documents planted on a corpse, and had to make use of the island's rugged landscape to escape to Italy

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster
programme.desc: A look at the strategy and series of errors that led to the sinking of the Bismarck in 1941, an incident during which more than 2,000 German sailors were killed

programme.title: Inside Melbourne Airport
programme.desc: At Australia's busiest airport which operates 24/7, a blackout halts operations and Resource Coordinator Andrew tries to restore power within an hour

programme.title: Hitler's Last Stand
programme.desc: Blinded in one eye in the battle for Normandy, a defiant French-Canadian sniper sets out to liberate a Dutch city in the final days of the war

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: During monsoon season at a treacherous tabletop runway in India, the crew of Flight 1344 runs off the tarmac and their Boeing 737-800 plunges into a gorge

programme.title: Apocalypse: The Rise of Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary charting the dictator's rise to power, from his beginnings as a struggling artist in Vienna to his eventual demise in Berlin. The first episode focuses on Hitler's formative years and how, when he returned home from the First World War, his nationalistic diatribes were largely ignored by the public, until the 1929 economic crisis put him and his ideas in the spotlight

programme.title: Apocalypse: The Rise of Hitler
programme.desc: Part two of two. Hitler becomes chancellor in 1933 after the German economy collapses and sets out to ensure the Nazi party's members dominate every aspect of society. One year later, he is proclaimed Fuhrer, beginning an ideological and military programme that eventually lead to the invasion of Poland and the start of the Second World War

programme.title: Secret Nazi Science
programme.desc: Exploring a history of groundbreaking ordnance, from a guided anti-ship gliding bomb to the world's first precision-guided weapon deployed during combat

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation

programme.desc: Documentary investigating the infamous disappearances of ships and aircraft, adopting the rather unorthodox technique of exploring the area's ominous reputation by 'draining the water from it' to see what exactly lies below the surface of the mythical triangle. Data from sophisticated sonar surveys enables the ocean floor to be seen, revealing strange geological features. The programme then contemplates whether they have had any influence on the mysterious occurrences that have been documented over the years

programme.title: Plane Crash Recreated
programme.desc: The events of Febtuary 25, 2009, when Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 went down less than a mile from the runway at Amsterdam Schiphol Airport, breaking into three pieces on impact and killing nine passengers and crew and all three pilots. Here, survivors tell their story of escaping from the wreckage of what should have been a normal commute home to Amsterdam

programme.title: Heroes of the Somme
programme.desc: Military historian Dr Gavin Hughes traces the stories of seven of the men whose remarkable bravery won them the Victoria Cross, Britain's most prized military medal. Hughes travels to France to piece together their experiences, covering four key days of the battle and telling the story of the entire offensive, using a copy of a 100-year-old battle map to navigate the Somme landscape today and pinpoint the locations of individuals and the battalions they fought in

programme.title: The World After Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver charts the brutal battles that marked the beginning of Roman Britain, discovering evidence of the empire's artillery and its first roads and forts. programme.title: WWI: The Last Tommies
programme.desc: In the concluding episode, veterans look back on the First World War battles of 1917 and 1918, and reflect on how Britain and her Allies turned a looming defeat into victory. programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate the case of Maria Roberts, the daughter of Italian parents who had a happy adoption growing up with an English family, and now hopes to find Gilda, her birth mother. The team also meets Francesca Barnes, who is in the early stages of transitioning from male to female. She was born with the name Paul and given up for adoption as a baby. Francesca is now searching for her mother, Norma, but fears her transition may prove too much of a challenge, and lead to rejection

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Growing up, Barbara Jacobs always believed that she was one of three children. It was not until she reached her twenties that she made a discovery which would change her life. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate Barbara's story, and also Sharon Hearte's. She was mixed race child adopted by a white family who grew up in the West Midlands. Sharon knew her birth mother, Barbara, was a white woman who worked in a Smethwick bicycle factory, and her father was from Jamaica. She wants to track down her mother to tell her that she understands why she was given up for adoption

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth takes a trip back in time when he visits a home that is dressed like a Victorian townhouse. Later, he helps a mother and daughter with an emotional task

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Alice Jones was brought up by her father, but on a visit to her mother more than 50 years ago she met her younger brother called Sam and has always hoped that one day she would see him again. Mark Ratcliffe grew up in Lancashire and at the age of 12 his parents told him he was adopted and the revelation devastated him. Ever since, he cannot shake the question of how a mother could give up her child and is now looking for answers

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Retired heptathlon star Jessica Ennis-Hill sets out to discover the truth behind a family mystery concerning her great-great-grandmother. When his father, Jess's great-grandfather was a child his mother Emily Maud, known as Maud, walked out. The family never talked about Maud, and Jess's grandfather has no idea what became of her. Jess wants to investigate what could have happened to make her two-time great-grandmother abandon her children and effectively be written out of the family's history. She also examines her Jamaican four-time great-grandfather's journey from enslavement to land ownership

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Angela Brennan believed she was adopted, but at 18 her mother revealed she had an older sister, also called Angela, who had been put up for adoption by her parents. When her son, Mark, was born, she did her best to raise him, but Bronwen found being responsible for a baby hard, and gave him up for adoption. She's always hoped she would see Mark again. Will she finally get her chance?

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Julie Roberts, a chocolate factory-worker from Birmingham, has always known she was adopted. She longs to find her mother Marion, to see what impact her teenage pregnancy had and find out what became of her. Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell also investigate the case of keen biker Marc Wolfe, who knows little about his birth mother, other than that she was Jewish and her one wish was for her son to be adopted into a Jewish family. programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Broadcaster Angela Scanlon teams up with comedian Kyla Cobbler to take on one of North Africa's most dramatic landscapes, the Atlas Mountains of Morocco. They face an ambitious week of climbing that quickly becomes a battle, not just with the terrain, but with Angela and Kyla's shared fear of heights

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The team is interrupted when Maigret is ordered to investigate death threats received by a billionaire businessman - Ferdinand Fumal

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.desc: The story of Dublin solicitor Thomas Byrne, who stole ?52 million from the banks and took ownership of 12 of his victims' houses in one of the biggest cases of white-collar crime in the history of the Irish State. This documentary charts how the solicitor scammed his neighbours and clients by transferring a dozen properties into his own name and lending from multiple banks using the houses as collateral

programme.title: Daniel O'Connell: The Emancipator
programme.desc: The year 2025 marked the 250th anniversary of the birth of Daniel O'Connell, the Kerry barrister and MP who became one of Ireland's greatest figures by securing Catholic emancipation, pioneering non-violent mass political mobilisation, and inspiring democratic movements around the world. This documentary tells his story, with insights by leading historians and scholars, including former President Mary McAleese, Declan Kiberd, Jay Rosman, Patrick Geoghegan, Niamh Gallagher and Chanté Mouton-Kinyon. Narrated by Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Cameras follow Polish gym owner Pawel as he and his daughter chase her MMA dreams, and street photographer Aarif as he documents rising tensions in the city centre. Architect Freidrich shares his vision for the redevelopment of Moore Street, while relatives of the 1916 leaders put forward their own plan

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Builder Harrison Gardner helps obstetrician Joan Lennon and her German partner Andrea Kowarschik, who are currently living in a campervan beside Joan's home town of Belturbet, Co Cavan, while they renovate two adjoining stone cottages, adding a timber frame extension to the rear for office space

programme.title: Kerry: Tides of Time
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the county's landscape, from its earliest origins, deep in the geological past, right up to the present day, told by those who know it intimately. This edition starts with a look at an isolated stretch of rocky shoreline that reveals evidence of the very first vertebrate life on Earth, before exploring human culture in the county

programme.desc: Republic of Ireland v Qatar (Kick-off 7.45pm). Peter Collins presents all the action from the match at Aviva Stadium. The Republic are looking to bounce back from the disappointment of losing on penalties to Czechia in the semi-finals of the FIFA World Cup play-offs and build towards the UEFA Nations League, which gets under way this October. Qatar did manage to qualify for the World Cup and will see this match as ideal preparation before their opener against Switzerland in Santa Clara on June 13

programme.title: Liam Brady: The Irishman Abroad
programme.desc: An intimate portrait exploring the highs and lows of Liam Brady's life and career, originally shown on the 50th anniversary of him signing schoolboy forms at Arsenal. programme.title: Art Traffickers: Treasures Stolen from the Tombs
programme.desc: Italy is home to the one of the most important artistic heritages in the world, but it is also where tens of thousands of precious archaeological finds are illegally trafficked every year, making it one of the world's biggest illicit industries. programme.desc: As Rhaenyra struggles to come to terms with her son's murder, Alicent grows concerned by Aegon's Small Council. Return of the fantasy drama set 200 years before the events of Game of Thrones and telling the story of House Targaryen. Starring Matt Smith and Emma D'Arcy

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra questions Daemon's loyalty as Otto schemes to turn the public against her, while Ser Criston Cole concocts a misguided plan for revenge

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: The team investigate the killing of an Asian shopkeeper who tried to forbid dope dealers from operating outside his store. His killing leads detectives to Mahoney, whose parting shot to Kellerman throws the detective into a tailspin of depression

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton and Lewis are assigned to the murder of a wealthy woman. Each focuses on a different suspect - the victim's brother and an angry, talented art student from the poor side of town. A diamond ring with great sentimental value disappeared from the woman's finger after the body was found, and proves pivotal to the case

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores the history of Bristol, beginning by learning a grisly secret at the docks before meeting Mercury Prize-winning musician Roni Size to discuss life in the St Pauls and Stokes Croft areas of the city. He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the world's highest swimming pool

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street

programme.title: Deadwood

programme.title: Deadwood

Drama, guest starring Gale Harold, with Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to New Orleans and meets soul singer Irma Thomas at the Hotel de Monteleone, an establishment that was popular with writers such as Tennessee Williams, F Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway. Richard speaks to Steel Magnolias author Robert Harling about the Soniat House, check into the supposedly haunted Hotel Maison de Ville and talks to burlesque dancer Chrissy Owens at the Roosevelt

programme.desc: Lulu faces the college board and must make a decision about her future, and Martin discovers Hilton's plans for the research and tries to warn Les before their life's work is lost

After sifting through the contents of Clark's trailer, Navarro and Danvers seek out a local hairdresser for insight on Annie, which leads to a tip about another former Tsalal worker. Later, tensions grow between Danvers and her stepdaughter Leah

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: On election night, Tom tries to manage an increasingly chaotic newsroom, while the Roy siblings spar over the outcome. Starring Jeremy Strong, Sarah Snook and Kieran Culkin

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Munch's high school love interest, Helen Rosenthal, is raped and murdered, leaving Munch and Kellerman trying to solve the case

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: As Ava embarks on her next project, Deborah, Marcus and Marty officially open The Diva, and Jimmy's new role proves fortuitous. On the heels of accomplishing everything she's ever wanted, Deborah reveals her final chapter. Final episode of the comedy drama, starring Jean Smart and Hannah Einbinder

programme.title: House of the Dragon

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Chris Thormann must confront the fact that the assailant who shot and blinded him is up for parole - and he might get it

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Glasgow, one of his favourite cities, to reveal unusual and often hidden sights and sounds overlooked by the public. Starting at the Necropolis, he travels to the world's oldest music hall before enjoying the views from a city-centre landmark. He also finds the time to take tea with a film star

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Chris Thormann must confront the fact that the assailant who shot and blinded him is up for parole - and he might get it

programme.title: The Meg (2018)
programme.desc: A massive creature attacks a deep-sea submersible, leaving the crew of the disabled vessel trapped at the bottom of the Pacific. Rescue diver Jonas Taylor is hired to save the crew from a 75ft-long prehistoric shark, known as the Megalodon. Director Jon Turteltaub's action thriller, starring Jason Statham, Bingbing Li, Rainn Wilson and Ruby Rose

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire (2014)
programme.desc: Ruler of the Persian empire and self-proclaimed god Xerxes continues his campaign to crush the emerging Greek nation, forcing a general from the democratic city state of Athens to lead his navy into battle against Xerxes' vast armada. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Sullivan Stapleton, Eva Green and Lena Headey

programme.title: The Hurricane Heist (2018)
programme.desc: Thieves attempt a major heist against the US Treasury as a Category 5 hurricane approaches one of its mint facilities. Action thriller, starring Toby Kebbell, Maggie Grace and Ryan Kwanten.

programme.title: Transformers: Dark of the Moon (2011)
programme.desc: The heroic autobots discover that a spaceship from their own planet crashed into the moon decades ago, carrying their former leader and an incredibly powerful device. They must fight to defend the human race as their enemies the decepticons plot to use this machine to rebuild their own world and destroy the Earth. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with Shia LaBeouf, Josh Duhamel and Rosie Huntington-Whiteley

programme.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots are driven into hiding when the human race turns against them. When rediscovered by a kindly inventor and his daughter, they come out of exile to save the world, battling a new breed of human-created Transformers. These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire (2014)
programme.desc: Ruler of the Persian empire and self-proclaimed god Xerxes continues his campaign to crush the emerging Greek nation, forcing a general from the democratic city state of Athens to lead his navy into battle against Xerxes' vast armada. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Sullivan Stapleton, Eva Green and Lena Headey

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom (2018)
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of Jurassic World, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing are asked to return to the theme park to save its resident dinosaurs from the island's active volcano. During their mission, they uncover a conspiracy that may affect the entire planet. Action sequel, starring Chris Pratt, Bryce Dallas Howard, Rafe Spall and Toby Jones

programme.title: Bumblebee (2018)
programme.desc: In 1987, lonely Californian teenager Charlie Watson is traumatised by the death of her father and resentful of her mother Sally's relationship with new boyfriend Ronald. For her 18th-birthday she's given a yellow Volkswagen Beetle, which turns out to be the battle-scarred Autobot Bumblebee. Transformers prequel, starring Hailee Steinfeld, John Cena and Jorge Lendeborg Jr

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island (2017)
programme.desc: Years after two enemy pilots land on a mysterious unchartered island in the Pacific, a team of scientists embark on a top-secret mission to the same region. Things soon descend into chaos when the local wildlife proves far from welcoming, and they must battle to escape from the domain of the mighty Kong. Action adventure, starring Tom Hiddleston, Samuel L Jackson and Brie Larson

He trains to use the incredible powers the ring grants him, and returns to Earth to defend it from a powerful force that feeds on fear. Sci-fi adventure, starring Ryan Reynolds, Blake Lively, Peter Sarsgaard and Mark Strong

Director Jon Turteltaub's action thriller, starring Jason Statham, Bingbing Li, Rainn Wilson and Ruby Rose

programme.title: Wonder Woman (2017)
programme.desc: An Amazon princess who grows up in enchanted realm has her first encounter with the outside world during the First World War. American airman Steve Trevor crash lands on her isolated island home. Determined to bring the chaos sweeping the world to an end, she ventures onto the front line in an attempt to bring down an ancient and powerful evil force that is driving the human race towards destruction. Director Patty Jenkins' blockbusting fantasy adventure, starring Gal Gadot, Chris Pine and David Thewlis

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017)
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Shazam! (2019)
programme.desc: Youngster Billy Batson is chosen by the ancient wizard Shazam as his new champion, and receives various superpowers. Together with his best friend Freddy Freeman, Billy must come to terms with his new talents in order to stop the evil Dr Thaddeus Sivana. Comedy adventure based on the Captain Marvel and Shazam comics, starring Zachary Levi, Mark Strong and Asher Angel

programme.title: The Legend of Tarzan (2016)
programme.desc: Economic unrest ensues after King Leopold cuts off access to the Belgian Congo, so years after leaving his old home behind, Tarzan is lured back from Victorian England hoping to stabilise matters. However, he soon finds he has been lured into a deadly trap orchestrated by one of Leopold's envoys. Adventure, starring Alexander Skarsgard, Margot Robbie and Samuel L Jackson

programme.desc: A cocky test pilot is given a mysterious ring by a dying alien and transported to another world, where he learns he has been recruited into a galactic peacekeeping organisation. He trains to use the incredible powers the ring grants him, and returns to Earth to defend it from a powerful force that feeds on fear. Sci-fi adventure, starring Ryan Reynolds, Blake Lively, Peter Sarsgaard and Mark Strong

programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro

programme.title: We Bury the Dead (2024)
programme.desc: When an experimental military weapon accidentally devastates Tasmania, volunteers travel to the island to help authorities identify the dead. Among them is a Ava Newman, who secretly hopes to find her missing husband. However, some of the bodies seem to be returning to life. Horror, starring Daisy Ridley, Brenton Thwaites and Mark Coles Smith

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: The Cinema List: Race Against Time
programme.desc: This edition is about the race against time, from a deadly threat in Fuze to The Desperate Hour, where Naomi Watts faces every parent's nightmare - the clock is ticking

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw 'acquires' an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennell's drama based on Emily Bronte's romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie (2025)
programme.desc: When Gabby's prized dollhouse ends up with eccentric cat lady Vera, the youngster embarks on a adventure to reunite the Gabby Cats and retrieve her beloved diorama before it's too late. Comedy adventure, starring Laila Lockhart Kraner and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: The Running Man (2025)
programme.desc: Desperate to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards enters a deadly televised competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins. Director Edgar Wright's comedy thriller based on the Stephen King story, starring Glen Powell, Lee Pace and Josh Brolin

programme.title: One Last Deal (2026)
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent scrambles to land one last high-stakes deal for his star client, with both their futures on the line. Drama, starring Danny Dyer and Elliott Rogers

programme.title: The Bride! (2026)
programme.desc: In 1930s Chicago, maverick scientist Dr Euphronius gives life to a murdered woman, sparking romance, police interest and radical change. Writer/director Maggie Gyllenhaal's horror based on The Bride of Frankenstein, starring Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale and Annette Bening

programme.title: Honey Don't (2025)
programme.desc: California private detective Honey O'Donahue delves into a series of unusual deaths connected to a mysterious church. Co-writer/director Ethan Coen's comedy mystery, starring Margaret Qualley, Aubrey Plaza and Chris Evans

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.title: The Shadow's Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the police's state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.desc: The story of Michael Danaher, who found himself deep in debt, prompting him to draw up a list of famous and wealthy robbery targets. But his plans resulted in vicious murder

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Father-and-son dog handlers John and Kevin Tozer join forces with their Alsatians Charlie and Floyd as the Metropolitan Police dog unit raids a suspected cannabis den. However, retirement beckons for John's canine companion, leaving 13-month-old Herbie as a possible replacement - if he can make it through basic training to join London's dog squad elite ranks. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: An investigation into the last pitched battle to have taken place on British soil, the Battle of Culloden in 1746, on the outskirts of Inverness. Rick Edwards and river detectorist Beau Ouimette search for clues as to where Bonnie Prince Charlie, crossed the River Nairn as he fled the battlefield pursued by government forces. The battle was to have an impact for decades to follow on those on the losing side, the Jacobites who wanted to restore the Stuart lineage to the British throne. The Jacobite forces were shot down as they carried out their famous Highland Charge across the moorland, and some of those who survived escaped and crossed the River Nairn in an attempt to return to the relative safety of their homelands

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallace's army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: Mysterious industrial waste and abandoned homes are discovered in a remote, uninhabited part of Greenland. In Norway, strange ditches and huge mounds are found deep within the Arctic Circle. A huge pyramid rivalling those of ancient Egypt is found far out on the Kazakh steppe. Plus, a perfectly shaped ball of golden fur melts out of the frozen ground of the Yukon's gold fields

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: The Hudson Valley area of upstate New York, where more than 7,000 UFO sightings have been reported. This edition speculates whether the area is home to one of the many extra-terrestrial gateways that are believed to be spread all over the world

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette head to north Wales to investigate waterways that may contain signs of the medieval Welsh Rebellion. Conwy Castle was constructed by Edward I between 1283-1287 after the Welsh Rebellion of 1282-3. The castle was part of a chain of fortifications known as the 'Iron Ring of Castles' and was both a material and symbolic sign of English power and dominance. Edward I selected its site on a high rock perched above the estuary of the River Conwy so that the castle could be supplied by the river in times of siege. Day two finds the pair at Ruthin Castle, a medieval fortress overlooking the River Clwyd

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A Kazakh farmer discovers ancient burial sites that may determine whether or not the legend of the Amazon women warriors is myth or reality. In the Italian Alps, a group of hikers explores long forgotten military infrastructure that points to the region's complicated and violent history. A shrine in the Indian Himalayas has long been said to be protected by supernatural powers, but strange markings on the temple reveal it was subject to an almost equally as powerful force. Palaeontologists discover a fossilised species unknown to science embedded in a rock hundreds of millions of years old

programme.title: Canadian Pickers

programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette look for evidence of Roman life in Scotland, heading to Ardoch Fort in Perthshire, on the east bank of the River Knaik. It spans over eight and a half acres and was built under Agricola around 80AD on the east bank of the River Knaik. The fort occupied a key strategic route through Perthshire and into north-east Scotland from the First to Third centuries

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. programme.title: Journey 2: The Mysterious Island (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager picks up a distress signal originating from the middle of the ocean. Joining an expedition to track the signal to its source, he discovers an unexplored island inhabited by strange creatures, and searches for a way to rescue its sole human inhabitant. Fantasy adventure sequel to Journey to the Centre of the Earth, starring Dwayne Johnson, Michael Caine and Josh Hutcherson

programme.title: Robin and the Hoods (2024)
programme.desc: For the tenacious 11-year-old Robin and her loyal band of friends, the Hoods, the patch of overgrown scrubland at the end of their cul-de-sac is a magical kingdom. However, when the slippery property developer Clipboard dazzles the parents of the area with promises of health spas and day-care facilities, the gang risk losing their kingdom forever. Director Phil Hawkins' comedy, starring Naomie Harris, Darcey Ewart, Gwendoline Christie and Mark Williams

programme.title: The Amazing Maurice (2022)
programme.desc: A streetwise cat has the perfect money-making scam after finding a pipe-playing kid who has a horde of strangely literate rats. Animated adventure based on the Terry Pratchett story, featuring the voices of Hugh Laurie, Emilia Clarke and David Thewlis

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: The Smurfs (2011)
programme.desc: The tiny blue forest-dwelling creatures flee from an evil wizard, only to end up magically transported to modern-day New York. They search for a way to get back to their own village while evading their enemy, who has followed them to the Big Apple. Fantasy comedy, with Neil Patrick Harris, Hank Azaria and Jayma Mays, plus voices of Jonathan Winters and Katy Perry

programme.title: The Smurfs 2 (2013)
programme.desc: The tiny blue forest-dwelling creatures and their human friends go on a rescue mission to Paris. Their old foe Gargamel the evil wizard has created mischievous smurfs of his own and kidnapped Smurfette to learn the secret of her magic. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Hank Azaria and Neil Patrick Harris, with the voices of Katy Perry and Christina Ricci

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 3: A Monster Vacation (2018)
programme.desc: Count Dracula and company participate in a cruise for sea-loving monsters, unaware that their boat is being commandeered by the monster-hating Van Helsing family. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Adam Sandler, Andy Samberg and Selena Gomez

programme.title: The Last Airbender (2010)
programme.desc: A chosen one with mastery of the four elements is charged with keeping the peace between four nations. When he disappears while still a boy, one of these factions sees a chance to gain absolute power and wages war on the others. After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.

programme.title: The Smurfs (2011)
programme.desc: The tiny blue forest-dwelling creatures flee from an evil wizard, only to end up magically transported to modern-day New York. They search for a way to get back to their own village while evading their enemy, who has followed them to the Big Apple. Fantasy comedy, with Neil Patrick Harris, Hank Azaria and Jayma Mays, plus voices of Jonathan Winters and Katy Perry

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
programme.desc: An unassuming hobbit is plunged into a battle against the forces of darkness when his uncle bequeaths him a magical ring of immense power. He joins a fellowship of heroes to embark on a perilous quest to destroy the ring before it falls into the hands of its creator - a dark lord who needs it to regain his power and conquer the world. The first part of Peter Jackson's epic fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen, Viggo Mortensen, Sean Astin, Christopher Lee, Liv Tyler and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Smurfs 2 (2013)
programme.desc: The tiny blue forest-dwelling creatures and their human friends go on a rescue mission to Paris. Their old foe Gargamel the evil wizard has created mischievous smurfs of his own and kidnapped Smurfette to learn the secret of her magic. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Hank Azaria and Neil Patrick Harris, with the voices of Katy Perry and Christina Ricci

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: Rebellious (2024)
programme.desc: A princess is kidnapped by an evil sorcerer and her scholarly fiance must use his wits in an epic quest to find her, while she boldly challenges her captors and plots a daring escape. Animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Vanessa Johansson and Marc Thompson

programme.title: Pil's Adventures (2021)
programme.desc: Pil, a young orphan living on the streets of Roc-en-Brume with her three tame weasels, disguises herself and embarks on a quest to save the heir to the throne and victim of an enchantment. Animated adventure, with the voice of Eleanor Noble

programme.title: Jack the Giant Slayer (2013)
programme.desc: A farm boy accidentally rebuilds the link between the human world and a kingdom of man-eating giants in the sky, resulting in a princess being trapped in the monsters' realm. He joins a band of the king's knights on a rescue mission, but one of the group intends to betray them in a bid to seize the throne. Fantasy adventure, starring Nicholas Hoult and Ewan McGregor

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Hook (1991)
programme.desc: Peter Pan, who has finally grown up and is now a married lawyer with a family of his own, is whisked away by Tinkerbell to Neverland, where he must learn to fly again if he is to save his children from the clutches of Captain Hook. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith

Meanwhile, their allies prepare to make a desperate last stand against the vast armies of the corrupted wizard Saruman, who has declared war on a neighbouring kingdom. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Sean Astin, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Billy Boyd, Dominic Monaghan, Andy Serkis and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)

Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen and Sean Astin

programme.desc: Comórtas Peile na Gaeltachta is widely regarded as one of the most important annual events for Irish speakers and Gaelic football fans alike. Cumann Peile Chlann na nGael from Ráth Chairn and Áth Buí in Meath hosted the event in 2025. This documentary follows the players, managers and supporters of various teams as they strive to win the 2025 tournament and have their name inscribed in Gaeltacht folklore. It also observes the festival organisers as they seek to successfully host this unique football and cultural event

Andrew Sachs guest stars, with George Cole and Gary Webster

Now they're designing and building a new, one-of-a-kind floating home for themselves - a futuristic-looking floating pod, drawing on a budget of £70,000

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam head to Leicester to create a garden for visually impaired Luigi and his wife Julie. The couple need a garden that is safe for Luigi to get around without any trip hazards and have plenty of shade for him to relax with family and friends. Charlie and Flo set out to transform the uneven, higgledy piggledy plot into the ideal space on a budget of £6,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill are in Congleton, Cheshire, to transform an outdoor space that looks more like a prison yard than the pretty cottage garden that owner Marie would like

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of locals living along Yorkshire's coastline. This episode features members of the Robson family from Bridlington, who've been fishing the North Sea for generations, as they head up the coast to Whitby for a boat repair. Volunteers at Bempton Cliffs work to protect and preserve almost half a million seabirds, and a group of sea swimmers take a full-moon sea swim at Scarborough

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: The bidders compete for two vintage Hermes handbags and a print by street artist Invader sells for 100 times what the seller paid for it. A 1970s illuminated furniture shop sign sells on the flip of a coin and a skateboard designed by Virgil Abloh goes up for sale

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: A reinforced concrete fire surround is given an overhaul with the restoration of the original quarry-tiled hearth

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave Wellman is in Romford tackling a bouncy wooden floor with wood worm and lays an oak floor

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Alan and Val McCoy put their life savings on the line to make their dream of living on water come true, entrusting a boat builder to make them their dream narrow boat floating home. The couple have been together for 11 years and now that they're retired, they've decided that owning a narrow boat could be a unique way to be near their families - all of whom live along the canal system across the Midlands and north-west England

programme.title: Highclere: The Real Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The team prepares to host their first charity gala in three years in the castle's iconic library. The event involves a clay pigeon shooting competition, followed by a three-course lunch and a big-ticket auction, but staff shortages put them under pressure to deliver the top-notch service that will encourage the guests to dig deep

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke are creating their latest tipple from tasty foraged seaside ingredients. In Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp, while newbie hoteliers Steph and Luke prepare for the busiest weekend of the summer season. Down the coast in Filey, military vehicle restorer Duncan preps his Second World War American Jeep for parade duty

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam head to Leicester to create a garden for visually impaired Luigi and his wife Julie. The couple need a garden that is safe for Luigi to get around without any trip hazards and have plenty of shade for him to relax with family and friends. Charlie and Flo set out to transform the uneven, higgledy piggledy plot into the ideal space on a budget of £6,000

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill redesign Iqbal and Pardeep's empty garden in Oldbury, Worcestershire, to reflect their love of sport and the outdoors while also providing a space for their teenage children to spend time with their friends or for family parties

programme.title: On the Game (1974)
programme.desc: A naughty and amusing look at the history of prostitution from cave women to Christine Keeler. Comedy, starring Lloyd Lamble and Gloria Maley.

programme.title: Permissive (1970)
programme.desc: A woman comes to London to visit an old friend, and gets drawn into the world of rock band groupies. She quickly sinks into a self-destructive hedonistic lifestyle, and is consumed with jealousy as she and rival groupies bitterly compete to outshine each other in the eyes of the musicians they cling to. Drama, starring Maggie Stride and Gay Singleton

programme.title: Musketeer
programme.desc: An old musketeer meets a youth and two scoundrels on the road to Paris. Short drama, starring Kevin McNally, Andrew Lewis and Charlie Allen

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
