programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill arrive in Bolton, Greater Manchester, where they set about transforming Debbie's tired and overgrown plot into a relaxing Southeast Asian-inspired haven, a perfect spot to remember her late father and practise yoga. For her £3,000 budget, Debbie would like to share her garden with as much wildlife as possible and would also love a natural pond

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill redesign Iqbal and Pardeep's empty garden in Oldbury, Worcestershire, to reflect their love of sport and the outdoors while also providing a space for their teenage children to spend time with their friends or for family parties

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke are creating their latest tipple from tasty foraged seaside ingredients. In Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp, while newbie hoteliers Steph and Luke prepare for the busiest weekend of the summer season. Down the coast in Filey, military vehicle restorer Duncan preps his Second World War American Jeep for parade duty

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: AJ Odudu hosts as buyers bid for the neon sign of a Soho sex shop and a piece of the wedding cake of William and Catherine, the Prince and Princess of Wales. Plus, one of only five remaining copies of Henry Beck's redesign of the London Tube map dating back to the 1930s

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave is in Gosport knocking down an internal wall to turn a property's outside toilet into a cloakroom

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Reclaimed bricks are used during a masterclass in old-English brickwork from the ground up

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: This edition charts the exploits of Pat and Sue Willan, who now retired plan to buy their very last floating home, which they hope to keep moored on the Thames. They have enlisted the help of a boat-maker in Stoke and opted for a 35-tonne, 55-foot state-of-the-art, all-mod-cons Dutch barge. They have a healthy budget of over £400,000 and elaborate plans for very contemporary kitchen fittings and gadgets and a powerful hydraulic engine

programme.title: Highclere: The Real Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house holds a celebration of the centenary of the exhibition that discovered the tomb of Tutankhamen, led by the great-grandfather of the current Lord Carnarvon. Lady Carnarvon is writing a book about her husband's famous ancestor, while housekeepers Sheena and Jason are cleaning the Egyptian museum in the basement. Once the domain of the maids and footmen, it now houses replicas from the tomb of Tutankhamen and is a treasure trove that requires them to use special cleaning techniques

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Fisherman Darren heads into the North Sea from Staithes in search of lobster and crab. Shellfish are now a mainstay of the catch on the Yorkshire coast, with conservationists ensuring that the lobster population remains buoyant by breeding and releasing them from a purpose-built hatchery in Whitby. Plus, a new recruit joins the team running the historic tramway in Saltburn, and an ambitious couple launch the first wine grown in their Robin Hood's Bay vineyard

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill arrive in Bolton, Greater Manchester, where they set about transforming Debbie's tired and overgrown plot into a relaxing Southeast Asian-inspired haven, a perfect spot to remember her late father and practise yoga. For her £3,000 budget, Debbie would like to share her garden with as much wildlife as possible and would also love a natural pond

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull transform Linda's front garden full of weeds and cobble to give her a Japanese-inspired garden that exudes elegance and drama and is a much better view for when she's staring out the window while working from home

programme.title: Sex with the Stars (1980)
programme.desc: A shy astrology columnist finds his life thrown into chaos when a sex-obsessed American takes over as editor and sets him an unusual assignment. He is ordered to sleep with 12 different women, one for each star sign, and write an article about each one. Comedy, starring Martin Burrows and Thick Wilson

programme.title: Secrets of a Door to Door Salesman (1973)
programme.desc: A young man gets a job as a vacuum salesman but finds that he has to fight off the advances of prospective female customers. Contains sex and nudity

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Caribbean cops enter the high-pressure world of fine dining when a famous chef is found stabbed to death the morning after the opening of his new restaurant. It proves to be a tricky investigation as the body was stored in a freezer, so the time of death cannot be identified - and when it turns out he was quite a hot-head in the kitchen, all his staff come under suspicion. Away from the case, Dwayne meets his match in the shape of a troublesome schoolboy, Florence has one last lesson in love for the inspector and JP makes a life-changing decision. Tara FitzGerald and Martin Compston guest star

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Charlie lets Rex take the lead as they investigate a murder at a prestigious dog show

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Effie enlists Murdoch's help to defend a maid accused of killing the owner of a fashion label, while Violet's neighbourhood safety programme proves to be anything but safe

Drama, starring Simon Baker, Robin Tunney, Owain Yeoman and Amanda Righetti

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: Trapped in an isolated mansion during a storm, Charlie and Rex must uncover a potential killer after the hostess is drugged, while Sarah fights to keep her alive

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: The team investigates when a local bank manager is found dead in a baseball arena having fallen from an aircraft. DNA of a man named Mark Payne is discovered under the victim's fingernails, and Carrie's infallible memory connects the suspect to the deceased's less-than-helpful girlfriend

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A marine salvage team unearth the legendary Saint Marie silver, but their celebration comes to an abrupt halt when their captain is found dead on the beach with a stab wound to his chest. With the captain's son Sam, wife Naomi and assistant Addison having watertight alibis, attention turns to rival treasure hunter Newton Farrell. Meanwhile, Dwayne is on best man duty as he organises the ultimate stag party for a terrified JP, and Florence is determined to find out what Humphrey got up to the night before his own wedding. Caribbean crime drama, guest starring Jack Donnelly (Atlantis) and Jill Halfpenny (Humans)

But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. When the deceased's father arrives at the precinct, taking hostages and creating a stand-off, the suspended officer gives himself over to the negotiator in a bid to defuse the situation, while Carrie relies on her remarkable memory to unlock the secret of the missing gun and clear her colleague's name

programme.title: Saint-Pierre
programme.desc: Arch's investigation into Gallagher takes a turn when his son becomes the prime suspect after a dead body washes up at a music festival

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A student backpacker is found dead in the showers of her hostel, and with a gun in her hand and her body slumped against the only door, all evidence points toward suicide. Humphrey isn't so sure, however, especially when he notices all her toiletries laid out and her towel missing. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a man is found dead inside a locked bank vault, Father Brown must solve the mystery to save an innocent man from paying the ultimate price. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.sub-title: The Fall of the House of Newsome

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis feature in a special edition of the topical comedy show featuring fond favourites, outtakes and other previously unseen bits

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are former Test Match Special commentator Henry Blofeld, Witless star Kerry Howard, broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and BBC News presenter Clive Myrie. They all reveal amazing stories, but are they telling the truth, or making it all up?

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Richard Osman, Lolly Adefope and Lee Mack join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'naked truth' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to make sense of the modern world, using a combination of stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction to dissect the week's news - with a panel of guests on hand to take Frankie to task over his claims

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Chris deals with community uproar over Jerry's new painting, which features Leslie in the nude. Ben tries to teach Andy and April about responsibility after he moves in with them. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Aziz Ansari, Rashida Jones and Nick Offerman

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Bake Off judge Prue Leith, reality TV star Scarlett Moffatt, rapper Big Narstie and weatherman Tomasz Schafernaker join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which the presenter takes a tour of the country, travelling from the islands of the Aegean to Athens. Along the way he meets local people including gun-toting priests and the last sponge-divers in the world, and discovers how they are coping with their country's current crises

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson looks at a range of Porsche 911s, James May gets behind the wheel of a classic Triumph TR6, and the small but powerful Clio V6 is pushed to the limit. Richard Hammond puts his case forward for the Land Rover as the greatest vehicle of all time, and Anne Robinson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and the team put track cars the Mitsubishi Evo VIII and the Subaru Impreza STi through their paces, Vauxhall's VX220 Turbo is road-tested and the search for the greatest car focuses on Stuart Hall's love of the Rolls-Royce Silver Cloud. Richard Whiteley is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the relationship between food and religion and celebrate the vast range of faith-based dishes available in Britain today. They rustle up simnel cake and brisket, as well as examining the history of the hot cross bun and dropping in on an Ethiopian Christmas feast

programme.desc: Professor Hannah Fry hosts an extended episode of the satirical quiz, as broadcaster Carol Vorderman and comedian Phil Wang join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news. Collectively, they poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian returns with his New World Order end of year special where, with the help of his guests, he'll dissect the bewildering year that was 2022. Frankie reviews and discusses all the big stories from the last 12 months along with a selection of unbelievable clips

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the 12th series of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Jo Brand, Olivia Colman, Stacey Dooley, Aston Merrygold, Diane Morgan, Bob Mortimer, Richard Osman, Lucy Porter, Jon Richardson, Henning Wehn and Shaun Williamson to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Deirdre O'Kane and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'non sequiturs' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico, following the work of the many officers who police the bridges and keep America safe. In the first episode, a shipment of cardboard packaging hides a deadly secret, and heroin disguised as a laptop baffles the world's most advanced narcotic detection device

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team take on a salvage mission of titanic proportions in very dangerous territory as they fight to shift a concrete yacht that has been underwater for 12 years.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team explores driving safety, and Jeremy Clarkson goes on manoeuvres in a Hummer - a military-style jeep - and Sweden's contribution to the world of supercars is revealed. Neil Morrissey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May and Richard Hammond look at convertibles, comparing the VW Beetle, Citroen Pluriel, Mercedes CLK, Mazda MX5, Daihatsu Copen and Audi Cabriolet. Jeremy Clarkson puts the Nissan 350Z through its paces, and supermodel Jodie Kidd is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the impact of Britain's colonial past on the nation's food, making a bread and butter pudding with chai and adding Indian pale ale to a sausage casserole. They also investigate the origins of gin and tonic and a brand of Worcester sauce, both of which arose from the UK's imperial past

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As Sam hovers on the brink of going into labour, Ricky struggles to confess to Bianca that he may be the baby's father. Ian presents Jane with an offer she cannot refuse

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: When a despotic soap opera director has his throat cut in the middle of a scene, the producer wants Harry and Fergus to find the culprit so filming can resume

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Lucien investigates when one of Ballarat's most prominent surgeons is found dead on his own operating table, and ends up being drawn into a web of jealousy and sexual intrigue. Meanwhile, as Chief Superintendent Munro's enmity towards the doctor continues, an old ally returns from Melbourne. Australian detective drama, starring Craig McLachlan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom decides to go on hunger strike at the Bayview Retirement Village until the standard of food improves. First episode of the sitcom, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The friends meet a mysterious man who has a machine that can locate 'hidden forces'

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio's curiosity is aroused when they encounter a man sporting an inflatable doll who wants to show the world his admiration for fat people and obesity, and hostility towards slimming and weight loss. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley is almost ready to unveil his latest creation, but his odd behavour makes the trio want to find out what lies within his shed sooner than he would prefer. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Gordon Wharmby star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: DI Mick Palmer, sole member of the Heritage Crime Unit, is dispatched to the imposing Astleigh House in Yorkshire, where art historian Francis Crozier has been murdered

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired detective Steve McAndrew arrives from Glasgow to help reinvestigate one of his original cases when a missing girl's DNA is found at the scene of a petrol station robbery in Clapham, south London. It's clear that the inquiry is a very personal one for McAndrew, and although his personality rubs the team up the wrong way, his methods soon pay dividends. Denis Lawson stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo investigate a series of murders, all of which seem connected to the Wetherton Arts Centre where a production of Oliver! is being rehearsed. However, the case takes an unexpected turn when the latest victim is a little closer to home. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist ventures into uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries, encountering extraordinary wildlife and meeting remarkable people. To begin, Steve and a team of climbers and jungle survival experts head into the ancient landscape of South America's Guiana Shield. This vast wilderness of pristine forests and towering table-top mountains is one of the most inaccessible terrains on the planet

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist leads an expedition into an unexplored desert canyon in the Jebel Akhdar mountain range of Oman. With temperatures soaring, dehydration and heat stroke are a constant threat

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of kayakers head to the Himalayas to attempt to row down the last great unexplored river in Bhutan. But there's a reason it has never been attempted before - it runs through a steep gorge which, according to legend, drops off the face of the planet

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.desc: Toby threatens to fire Madeline when he discovers she is still seeing Tom. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: Frank begrudgingly accompanies Margaret on a visit to his ex-wife, revealing to his daughter a side she never knew existed. Greg continues to downplay his chances with Anne-Marie, forcing Gabby to commence Operation Greg 2.0. Diane sniffs a lead before going on an unlikely date. John meets a cook, and tries crack. DCI Haines and DS Selforth build their case, leading to a near miss with the two youngest outlaws

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks' thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didn't do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.sub-title: If You Go Down to the Woods
programme.desc: Marlene is distraught when Tyler and Earl the dog go missing. Boycie, Bryan and Jed form a search party, but hunting through the haunted woods at night proves a less than pleasant experience for the would-be rescuers. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony decides to break up with his girlfriend and agonises over the best way to go about it. He unintentionally creates the ideal opportunity by cancelling a date to prepare a meal for Deborah. Starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch tries to ensure that Godber has not blown his chances of early release after he gets into a fight a week before his parole board hearing. Final episode of the classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene eagerly anticipates Tyler's A-level results, dreaming of a long and distinguished academic future for her son. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio scheme to get Wally out from under Nora Batty's thumb for a day, but come a cropper sneaking him back to the house. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Joe Gladwin

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The new controller wreaks havoc at Maplins as he sets about firing numerous members of staff, and upsets Peggy in the process. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Tipped off by Frazer and Godfrey, Mainwaring and Wilson investigate a house from where they believe someone may have been signalling to enemy aircraft. They take the suspect back to the church hall, but chaos ensues when an incendiary bomb comes through the roof and the platoon desperately battles the blaze. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock's wife is hired as Mr Rumbold's secretary, and he becomes convinced the two are having an adulterous liaison. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Nicholas Smith and Diana King

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.sub-title: An Errand Boy by the Ear
programme.desc: Arkwright decides Granville needs toughening up and starts dragging him around the store by his ear, but the regular customers are unhappy by what they see

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville's idea to open a new catering arm, offering his customers tasty toasties, doesn't quite go according to plan when his reconditioned toaster blows a fuse. Mr Newbold gets a little bolder in his pursuit of Mrs Rossi, while trying to avoid the Black Widow. Leroy and Willis meet Beth and Ruby with a view to going on a date, but Willis is worried that Ruby's not right for him. David Jason, James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Geoffrey Whitehead star in veteran writer Roy Clarke's much-loved comedy

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Tipped off by Frazer and Godfrey, Mainwaring and Wilson investigate a house from where they believe someone may have been signalling to enemy aircraft. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio scheme to get Wally out from under Nora Batty's thumb for a day, but come a cropper sneaking him back to the house. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Joe Gladwin

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio stumble across a health farm on a day out, leading Foggy to devise an ingenious money-making scam - selling food to its famished inhabitants. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth develops a passion for art, Rose acquires a new admirer, and Daddy has a yearning to join the Foreign Legion. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are challenged to a cricket match by Warden Hodges, who has signed up a professional for his side. Featuring a guest appearance by former England and Yorkshire fast bowler Fred Trueman. Vintage comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and Bill Pertwee

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright tries to improve the sale of an overstocked commodity - Jamaican Ginger cake - with incredible efficiency, and Granville's lust for power leads to disaster

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Tim and Kate's closeness following the death of his grandmother becomes a cause for concern for Lee, so he shares his worries with a counsellor - who is unimpressed by his lack of social skills. The subsequent funeral descends into chaos, thanks to an irate elderly relative and an addictive drink

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee pretends to be gay to find out more about a new business acquaintance - but only because his reward for doing so is tickets to Wembley. His deception is soon uncovered and he is taken to a 'specialist' club to test his alleged sexuality. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright tries to improve the sale of an overstocked commodity - Jamaican Ginger cake - with incredible efficiency, and Granville's lust for power leads to disaster

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom and Toby disagree over the private and national health systems. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dorothy welcomes Gary's suggestion of an open relationship, while Dermot continues to woo Deborah

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary goes to the pub to drown his sorrows, so Dermot invites Deborah to their flat for a romantic evening of candlelight, dinner and music. Laddish comedy, starring Harry Enfield and Martin Clunes

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

programme.desc: Guy's constant presence in the flat continues to rile Lee and and his distrust is fuelled further when he discovers his unwanted housemate runs a lap-dancing club. programme.sub-title: How to Ignite Your Errand Boy

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin and Perry try to befriend some girls at a party, while Tim Nice-But-Dim transforms himself into a rock star, and Lance sinks into depression when his imaginary girlfriend dumps him. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Still Open All Hours

Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee clashes with a youth when he gets a job distributing leaflets in a shopping centre, and is persuaded to pretend to be Tim in front of Kate's Australian friend Ruth, who has landed on their doorstep. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Megan Dodds

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy's constant presence in the flat continues to rile Lee and and his distrust is fuelled further when he discovers his unwanted housemate runs a lap-dancing club. programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Not Going Out

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Kevin and Perry try to befriend some girls at a party, while Tim Nice-But-Dim transforms himself into a rock star, and Lance sinks into depression when his imaginary girlfriend dumps him. Comedy sketches, featuring Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The veteran broadcaster follows life inside two prisons holding some of America's most notorious female criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility in the same US state. Among the inmates he meets in the first edition are Sarah Pender, who went on the run while serving 110 years for double murder, before being captured four months later, and Martha Search, who is looking forward to getting to grips with modern technology when her 20-year sentence comes to an end

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: An active mum from a tight-knit pro wrestling family trades places with a mother-of-three whose husband prefers playing video games to spending time with his family

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Dominic Harrison - aka singer and musician Yungblud - as he joins him during his whirlwind tour. Along the way, Louis meets fans who follow Yungblud with almost religious intensity, before sitting down with the singer to discuss a range of topics including his mental health, his sexuality and his response to outspoken critics

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: It's a race for the last spot in the immunity cook! There are four cloches with secret flavour bombs left by Jamie Oliver - but they won't be revealed until the task begins

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert dons his hard hat as guest presenter for this ambitious build in Brandesburton near Hull, East Yorkshire. The team are joined by local volunteers to transform an overgrown 2.5 acres into a purpose-built adventure campsite for the St Michael's Youth Project (St Mike's), which receives funding from Children in Need

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Scunthorpe woman Caroline Blanchard, a single mum who has twice had to make the decision to turn off the life support for people she loves. She and her children live in the shadow of the garage, where seven-year-old Natasha banged her head in 2007, and the conservatory, where husband and father Paul had a heart attack four years later. The volunteers' plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: A brutal murder on Glasgow's canals hits close to home when the victim is identified as a former police officer

programme.title: How to Catch a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The veteran broadcaster meets more inmates inside two of Indiana's most dangerous women's prisons. Among them are Dawn Hopkins, who was sentenced to life for murdering her three-month-old son, and Sarah 'Cindy' White, who was incarcerated as a teenager and will spend the rest of her life behind bars. The journalist also hears the remarkable story of Linda Darby who, after escaping from jail in 1972, spent the next 35 years on the run - before the law finally caught up with her

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A cosmopolitan, appearance-focused mum-of-three switches places with the mum from a down-to-earth farming family who has four children and hundreds of animals

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a 49-year-old who has fallen in the street after a night out in Stoke, while in Dudley, paramedics visit a five-month-old who has a temperature

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows don't fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didn't give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called 'Wagatha Christie', which followed accuisations between footballers' wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: A detailed look at the life of Aaron Hernandez, a millionaire athlete with a fiancee and baby, who was shockingly convicted of murder

programme.desc: It's a race for the last spot in the immunity cook! There are four cloches with secret flavour bombs left by Jamie Oliver - but they won't be revealed until the task begins

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert dons his hard hat as guest presenter for this ambitious build in Brandesburton near Hull, East Yorkshire. The team are joined by local volunteers to transform an overgrown 2.5 acres into a purpose-built adventure campsite for the St Michael's Youth Project (St Mike's), which receives funding from Children in Need

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Scunthorpe woman Caroline Blanchard, a single mum who has twice had to make the decision to turn off the life support for people she loves. She and her children live in the shadow of the garage, where seven-year-old Natasha banged her head in 2007, and the conservatory, where husband and father Paul had a heart attack four years later. The volunteers' plan is to give them a new home for a new start, including demolishing the conservatory and garage where these two tragedies occurred

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: After 15 years of fostering, Stephen and Lynn Smedley were looking forward to their well-earned retirement in Carbis Bay, Cornwall. However, the dream dramatically changed when their daughter died, leaving them caring for their three grandsons. The boys, still grieving for their mum's untimely death, are forced to share a single bedroom with a triple bunkbed. There is only one bathroom and Lynn is forced to do the ironing in the shed due to lack of space. Enter Nick Knowles and the team. Their plan is to reconfigure and enlarge their home to accommodate three growing boys - with the help of hundreds of volunteers

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A couple bring their 13-month-old cocker spaniel puppy in for scheduled surgery for hip dysplasia which will require two separate full hip replacements. Elsewhere, two-year-old cat Jazz is rushed in by mum Sarah suffering from a smashed femur and shattered thorax after being hit by a car, and eight-year-old dachshund Olive is treated after suddenly losing the use of her back legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel diagnoses the joint condition osteochondritis dissecans in a hyperactive ridgeback named Loki, and discusses treatment options with his owners. Newfoundland Hugo is referred for his hip dysplasia, but Noel discovers issues with his elbow and knee joints, while Carol and Nigel bring their mischievous seven-month-old Havana kitten Zahra following a mysterious accident at home, which resulted in a broken leg

programme.title: DIY SOS

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: A brutal murder on Glasgow's canals hits close to home when the victim is identified as a former police officer

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: Stacey meets single mum-of-eight Marie Buchan, a lightning rod in the fierce debate over the UK benefits system who has openly shared her state-funded lifestyle

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The veteran broadcaster follows life inside two prisons holding some of America's most notorious female criminals - Indiana Women's Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility in the same US state. Among the inmates he meets in the first edition are Sarah Pender, who went on the run while serving 110 years for double murder, before being captured four months later, and Martha Search, who is looking forward to getting to grips with modern technology when her 20-year sentence comes to an end

programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: With access to key individuals who didn't give evidence at the trial, this documentary explores the hidden workings of the tabloids, PRs and agents connected to so-called 'Wagatha Christie', which followed accuisations between footballers' wives Coleen Rooney and Rebekah Vardy. Contributors include Colin Paterson and Lizzie Cundy

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A cosmopolitan, appearance-focused mum-of-three switches places with the mum from a down-to-earth farming family who has four children and hundreds of animals

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a 49-year-old who has fallen in the street after a night out in Stoke, while in Dudley, paramedics visit a five-month-old who has a temperature

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Four cooks vie for the chance to sit out Sunday's elimination. In the first round, contestants must produce a dish using the ingredients found in mentor Shannon Bennett's fridge

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An energetic one-year-old bull terrier puppy called Apple comes into the surgery with an ankle problem, and Noel hopes a custom-made, 3D-printed implant may give this beloved pet a new lease of life. He also treats a two-year-old Siamese cat with a broken leg, shattered pelvis and suspected spinal injury after being hit by a van, while four-year-old collie cross Rosco is tested for hip dysplasia

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A six-month-old collie appears to have a developmental deformity where the bones in its elbows don't fit together, but after CT scans, Noel sees that the situation is even more complex. A Burmese mountain dog has a painful bulging disc, which Noel hopes to replace with a bespoke titanium spacer screw, while cat Elmo has a complex fracture to its left back leg after being hit by a car

programme.title: How to Catch a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Former detective Steven Fulcher breaks down the risky investigation that caught infamous murderer Christopher Halliwell - and ended his policing career

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike hit a new obstacle to nailing down the next event booking when the Captain's past threatens to be unearthed, and he wildly protests at the couple's endeavours. Meanwhile, Thomas and Julian battle it out over how the ghosts will spend their evening, Lady Button is confused by urges she hasn't felt before, and an old friend of Kitty's reappears

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: A detailed look at the life of Aaron Hernandez, a millionaire athlete with a fiancee and baby, who was shockingly convicted of murder

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Canadian comedian, actor and writer, Katherine Ryan, for a frank and honest conversation about her career, relationships and the changing shape of her family. Louis accompanies Katherine as she performs her latest stand-up tour, Missus, in Southend-On-Sea, and later, he spends a rare day off with Katherine, her daughter Violet and her husband Bobby

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedics assess a man police have found acting erratically on a busy dual carriageway and believe has taken the powerful psychoactive Monkey Dust

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Contestants must choose one of three ingredients that have inspired the judges during their childhood, including Andy's Vegemite, Mel's cumquats or Jock's panettone

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, a man collapses while playing bingo two days before his wedding, while in Stoke, crews are called to help an elderly man who has had an unexplained fall.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Over the years Derek has learnt to always expect the unexpected. So when Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi drops off his Bedford CA campervan it's just another day at the office

programme.title: Classic Car Kings
programme.desc: Irv imports an original-run short wheelbase Porsche 911 from the east coast of America. He needs to make it roadworthy, while keeping its incredible sun-faded patina

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: Following a welcome break in Cyprus, HMS Queen Elizabeth heads back to sea, but after passing through the Suez Canal it's discovered that one sailor has contracted Covid-19 while on shore leave. A week later, as the ship enters the Indian Ocean, the vurus has spread almost out of control with 400 sailors now in isolation - either because they are infected or are close contacts with those who have tested positive. With almost a third of the ship's labour force out of commission, command has to find ways of keeping the aircraft carrier operational

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth continues her journey to the South China Sea, but rampant Covid on board means she is not allowed to come ashore for any of the planned port visits. Sailing past Singapore and Hong Kong, the ship has become a floating prison, isolated on the high seas. Her dedicated supply ships do their best to keep the Queen Elizabeth topped up with food, fuel and ammunition. As the QE and her escorting destroyers and frigates enter the contested and potentially dangerous waters of the South China Sea, the Chinese Navy react by probing her with submarines and surface warships. Anti-submarine helicopters launch continually in order to identify, track and see off the subsurface threat

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that 'goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications'

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off a Jensen Interceptor and a Jensen Healey along with a one-off Wood & Pickett Triumph Dolomite Sprint. Sarah takes a shine to a four-tonne road roller

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts head to the Scottish Borders to invite valuations at Abbotsford, near Melrose, once the home of Sir Walter Scott. Objects include a bottle used by smugglers to fool the customs men, a miniature battle scene exquisitely hand-cut by a First World War prisoner and a dinner service fit for a queen

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set sail for the Channel Islands, visiting Saumarez Park in Guernsey. Objects up for valuation include a piece of furniture used as target practice by the Germans during the occupation of the island, a giant sapphire once owned by a maharajah, and a single bank note worth significantly more than its face value

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek's on his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest priced item at the next auction. But, the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming explores the Victorian vWest Yorkshire village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. He also moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his epic journey across the north of England, narrowboater Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of Britain's inland waterways

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: In Shropshire a Great Western Railway 'Modified Hall' steam engine and its tender is being taken out of service as it needs a safety inspection

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Storm Arwen hits the UK and the skippers have tough decisions to make. Boats start to roll in the North Sea, putting Victory Rose and Our Lass at risk of collision. Skipper Darren has just three days of crabbing time before the storm pushes the boat back to the harbour and George Jack considers venturing in to the storm in search of a new species of prawn

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek's on his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest priced item at the next auction. But, the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek casts his eye over a Citroen 2CV, before he is off admiring the treasure trove of more than 270 motorbikes in the same garage

programme.title: Classic Car Kings
programme.desc: Irv is an expert in VW Sambas, so when a collector with a bunch of rusted panels and the dream of a showroom-quality vehicle needs help, there's only one man for the job

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel around Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a profit for their owners. In this episode, Henry finds a treat for restorer Guy, and Simon creates stunning table lamps from old theatre lights

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: The historian investigates the complex factors that shaped the dark imagination of a refined Devonshire lady, discovering family secrets and a childhood haunted by a sinister figure. Focusing on the first third of Agatha Christie's life, this episode unearths the surprising roots of her most compelling themes, the inspiration for some of her greatest creations, and the secrets that the enigmatic Christie herself kept carefully hidden from public view

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: After travellingmore than 20,000 miles, HMS Queen Elizabeth heads from Japan back to of the tropical island Guam for vital maintenance. Soon, however the ship and its escorts are back at sea and sailing towards the disputed waters of the China Sea, where they join up with other allied warships for a display of military might in front of the Chinese navy

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.sub-title: It's Not Over Till It's Over
programme.desc: After six months at sea on its first operational deployment, HMS Queen Elizabeth departs the Pacific for the long voyage home. After crossing the Arabian Sea, the ship stops at Duqm in Oman to replenish stores, before proceeding up the Red Sea, through the Suez canal and into the eastern Mediterranean. An F35 jet on a routine launch loses power at a critical moment and the pilot has to eject, and HMS Queen Elizabeth launches a search and rescue mission to locate the airman

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A 1971 Mercedes SL and a 1989 Jaguar XJS emerge from a barn on a chicken farm, a Roller and a Rover go under the hammer and a 1966 Triumph Spitfire is restored to near perfection

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek takes a shine to a rare 1958 Armstrong Siddeley Sapphire with a long family history and a convertible Triumph Herald brings a few smiles on auction day

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A mile-and-a half of recently-excavated trenches, bunkers, barracks, and gun encasements near Pointe du Hoc, which tell a new story of D-Day

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a special edition commemorating the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings, filmed in Normandy, France, and the D-Day Museum Portsmouth. The story of the landings are told through various items brought in by visitors and rare artefacts held in various museums across the UK, and there are eyewitness accounts from those who played a part, including radio operator Marie Scott and veteran Ken Scott

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents as the team visits Brooklands motor racing museum, near Weybridge in Surrey, where the items valued include aboriginal tools, a bust found abandoned in a garden and an old chest that was given away on the internet. The experts also examine artefacts documenting attempts to break the world land speed record

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: More valuations from the motor racing museum at Brooklands, near Weybridge in Surrey. Items include a rare stirrup cup, a set of books once owned by Winston Churchill, and a bracelet given to Queen Victoria that was found in a gutter. Fiona Bruce also welcomes the return of an old friend of the programme

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up an impressive five vehicle haul from a collector who can no longer look after his motors. Paul's out on the road collecting a rare 1972 Lancia Fulvia Berlina

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins a fresh set of adventures along the waterways of the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat the Naughty Lass while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire. He then cruises beneath Conisbrough's magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the Chesterfield Canal which threatens his voyage

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are drawn by the curves of a stunning 1999 Morgan with an incredible 4.6 V8 engine, but things aren't as good as they seem when they get to the garage

programme.desc: Dave's drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a '1970s beige' with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds two rare Fiats and a VW Corrado. Derek has his work cut out trying to remove an old Morris that appears to be supporting the garage roof. What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: Classic Car Kings

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to a private railway, where Simon converts a lantern to a stylish table and Henry turns a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christie's life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Mega Metro

programme.title: Classic Car Kings

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: Following a welcome break in Cyprus, HMS Queen Elizabeth heads back to sea, but after passing through the Suez Canal it's discovered that one sailor has contracted Covid-19 while on shore leave. A week later, as the ship enters the Indian Ocean, the vurus has spread almost out of control with 400 sailors now in isolation - either because they are infected or are close contacts with those who have tested positive. With almost a third of the ship's labour force out of commission, command has to find ways of keeping the aircraft carrier operational

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth continues her journey to the South China Sea, but rampant Covid on board means she is not allowed to come ashore for any of the planned port visits. Sailing past Singapore and Hong Kong, the ship has become a floating prison, isolated on the high seas. Her dedicated supply ships do their best to keep the Queen Elizabeth topped up with food, fuel and ammunition. As the QE and her escorting destroyers and frigates enter the contested and potentially dangerous waters of the South China Sea, the Chinese Navy react by probing her with submarines and surface warships. Anti-submarine helicopters launch continually in order to identify, track and see off the subsurface threat

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that 'goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications'

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off a Jensen Interceptor and a Jensen Healey along with a one-off Wood & Pickett Triumph Dolomite Sprint. Sarah takes a shine to a four-tonne road roller

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs' classic documentary explores the Second World War's powerful legacy. For many people the conflict was the most significant experience of their lives, but it also left millions dead - ordinary civilians and victims of persecution, as well as military casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts head to the Scottish Borders to invite valuations at Abbotsford, near Melrose, once the home of Sir Walter Scott. Objects include a bottle used by smugglers to fool the customs men, a miniature battle scene exquisitely hand-cut by a First World War prisoner and a dinner service fit for a queen

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team set sail for the Channel Islands, visiting Saumarez Park in Guernsey. Objects up for valuation include a piece of furniture used as target practice by the Germans during the occupation of the island, a giant sapphire once owned by a maharajah, and a single bank note worth significantly more than its face value

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: The historian investigates the complex factors that shaped the dark imagination of a refined Devonshire lady, discovering family secrets and a childhood haunted by a sinister figure. Focusing on the first third of Agatha Christie's life, this episode unearths the surprising roots of her most compelling themes, the inspiration for some of her greatest creations, and the secrets that the enigmatic Christie herself kept carefully hidden from public view

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.desc: After travellingmore than 20,000 miles, HMS Queen Elizabeth heads from Japan back to of the tropical island Guam for vital maintenance. Soon, however the ship and its escorts are back at sea and sailing towards the disputed waters of the China Sea, where they join up with other allied warships for a display of military might in front of the Chinese navy

programme.title: The Warship: Tour of Duty
programme.sub-title: It's Not Over Till It's Over
programme.desc: After six months at sea on its first operational deployment, HMS Queen Elizabeth departs the Pacific for the long voyage home. After crossing the Arabian Sea, the ship stops at Duqm in Oman to replenish stores, before proceeding up the Red Sea, through the Suez canal and into the eastern Mediterranean. An F35 jet on a routine launch loses power at a critical moment and the pilot has to eject, and HMS Queen Elizabeth launches a search and rescue mission to locate the airman

programme.title: Bangers & Cash

programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help six more people lose weight with different methods. Natasha and Jodian have just two weeks to slim down before a trip to Egypt, with one trying out a high-fat diet and the other eating Mediterranean food. In Hampshire, Mike tries out a regimen that consists almost entirely of specially-made cookies and Steve is on a volumetric diet consisting of large bowls of soup or massive salads, while Gina and Max in Wigan go on paleolithic and starch diets respectively. programme.desc: An elite handful of fish are capable of living and thriving in both fresh and saltwater. Among these specialists are aggressive man-eaters, venomous giants and a wickedly armed beast that hails from a bygone age. In this special edition of River Monsters, biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade relives his thrilling encounters with some of these fearsome intruders that swim in from beyond the surf. Jeremy reveals how our understanding of these predators has shifted dramatically in recent years and answers pertinent questions about what draws these ocean-dwelling hunters into freshwater in the first place and how they are capable of crossing what is normally an impenetrable barrier
